tasks.title: Wave 0: Architecture Decision Record — Pi v2+v3 unified design
tasks.description: Produce ADR-035 at .cleo/adrs/ADR-035-pi-v2-v3-harness.md covering architecture for all 9 features in the epic. Follows the existing ADR protocol (see ADR-034 as reference): zero-padded 3-digit number, Context/Decision/Consequences/Alternatives sections, Status+Date+relatedTasks header. Must resolve: (1) directory conventions for extensions/prompts/themes install (global vs project scope, precedence, conflict handling). (2) JSONL session parsing strategy (streaming vs load-all, resume semantics). (3) models.json vs settings.json:enabledModels — which is authoritative? (4) MCP-as-Pi-extension wire protocol — stdio-in-subprocess vs direct JSON-RPC vs shared runtime. (5) CANT .cant loading — parse-time vs run-time, via cant-core napi or TS parser. (6) Orchestrator spawn mechanics — streaming stdout, error propagation, session attribution. (7) v3 exclusivity switch — runtime detection, graceful degradation when Pi absent, migration story. MUST also append a manifest entry to .cleo/adrs/MANIFEST.jsonl with id=ADR-035, file=.cleo/adrs/ADR-035-pi-v2-v3-harness.md, status=accepted, date=YYYY-MM-DD, relatedTasks=[T261,T262], summary, keywords, topics. This task does research + context + architecture + specification work. The 9 downstream implementation tasks are the decomposition output.
acceptance: .cleo/adrs/ADR-035-pi-v2-v3-harness.md committed — follows ADR-034 protocol shape (Status/Date/relatedTasks header, Context/Decision/Consequences/Alternatives sections)
.cleo/adrs/MANIFEST.jsonl appended with the ADR-035 entry (id/file/title/status/date/relatedTasks/summary/keywords/topics)
Each of the 9 features has at least 1 concrete spec-level acceptance criterion ready to copy into its implementation task
Each of the 7 architecture questions above has a recorded decision with rationale
Cross-cutting concerns documented: file conflict handling, version migration, Pi-absent fallback behaviour
notes: 2026-04-07 13:35:48 UTC: This is the Wave 0 blocker — no Wave 1+ task can start until the ADR is merged. The ADR serves as the single source of truth that all downstream implementation tasks reference.
2026-04-07 13:45:59 UTC: Corrected path from the incorrect 'docs/adrs/' to the canonical '.cleo/adrs/' per the project's ADR protocol. Next available number is 035 (highest existing is 034).

tasks.title: @cleocode/cant TS loader for .cant files (napi-backed)
tasks.description: Add a typed loadCantFile(path) convenience export to the existing @cleocode/cant package (packages/cant/src/). Does NOT create a new package — extends the existing one. The function reads a .cant file from disk, calls the existing parseDocument and validateDocument napi bindings (crates/cant-napi exports cant_parse_document and cant_validate_document), combines their results, and returns a typed AST matching the Rust-side CantDocument shape from crates/cant-core/src/dsl/ast.rs. The returned type must cover all 8 Section variants (Agent, Skill, Hook, Workflow, Pipeline, Import, Binding, Comment), all 16 workflow Statement types, all 7 Value types (String, Number, Boolean, Array, Identifier, Duration, ProseBlock), and Expression nodes (Name, String with interpolations, Number, Boolean, Duration, TaskRef, Address, Array, PropertyAccess, Comparison, Logical, Negation, Interpolation). Error handling: parse errors throw with cant-core message and span info; validation errors throw with rule ID + message + line/col. Caches parsed ASTs by file mtime to avoid re-parsing unchanged files. This is small, additive, no new package — just a convenience wrapper over the existing parseDocument + validateDocument surface.
acceptance: loadCantFile(path) reads a .cant file and returns a typed AST matching CantDocument from crates/cant-core/src/dsl/ast.rs
Return type covers all 8 Section variants, 16 workflow Statement types, 7 Value types, and all Expression node variants from the Rust AST
ProseBlock nodes preserve multi-line content verbatim including indentation
Parse errors throw with cant-core error message, line, col, and severity fields from JsParseError
Validation errors throw with rule ID (e.g., 'S05', 'P06', 'W09'), message, severity, and location from JsDiagnostic
Cache parsed ASTs by file mtime; return cached AST if file unchanged
Unit tests parse all existing .cant fixture files (find via glob under crates/cant-core/fixtures/ and .cleo/agents/) and verify round-trip against Rust-side expected ASTs
TypeScript types exported from @cleocode/cant match the Rust JsParseDocumentResult shape 1:1 — no type drift
notes: 2026-04-07 16:53:18 UTC: 2026-04-07 15:55: Re-scoped per ADR-035 D5 audit. No new package. Extends existing @cleocode/cant with loadCantFile helper that wraps the already-shipped parseDocument + validateDocument. All type coverage must reference the 16 workflow statement types from crates/cant-core as the SoT — TS types cannot drift from Rust.

tasks.title: Wire WorkflowExecutor.evaluateCondition to real Expression evaluator — end the return-true stub
tasks.description: FIX: workflow-executor.ts lines 562-564 currently has this comment: '// For regular expressions, return true as a stub\n// Real implementation would evaluate the expression against the scope\nreturn true;'. This silently corrupts every if/elif/loop-until with a boolean condition — the true branch always wins. Implement the real evaluator that walks Expression AST nodes (all variants from crates/cant-core/src/dsl/ast.rs:404-436) against the ExecutionScope and returns the correct boolean judgement. Must honour validation rule T07 (no nested interpolation) and T02/T03 (comparison operand type compatibility). This is Wave 1 of T287 — blocks everything else because conditions are used by executeConditional, executeLoopUntil, and the eventual hook-dispatch logic in T295. Parent: T287.
acceptance: WorkflowExecutor.evaluateCondition at packages/core/src/cant/workflow-executor.ts:542-565 evaluates Expression AST nodes against the current scope — no more 'return true' stub

Supports all Expression variants from crates/cant-core/src/dsl/ast.rs:404-436: Name, String (with interpolations), Number, Boolean, Duration, TaskRef, Address, Array, PropertyAccess (chained dot), Comparison (==, !=, >, <, >=, <=), Logical (and, or), Negation (not), Interpolation (${expr})
Comparison operands type-coerce per T02/T03 validation rules — string, number, boolean compatible; duration comparisons in milliseconds
PropertyAccess walks chained keys: a.b.c resolves via scope then object traversal; throws with clear message on undefined path
Interpolation is single-pass per T07 validation rule — no nested ${} re-expansion
Discretion conditions still delegate to DiscretionEvaluator unchanged
Unit tests cover every Expression variant against a realistic ExecutionScope with nested objects
All 8 existing .cant fixture files still parse and validate after changes
notes: 2026-04-07 16:56:42 UTC: 2026-04-07 16:20: Created as Wave 1 subtask of T287. Root of the critical path — all other T287 subtasks become meaningful only once conditions actually evaluate.

tasks.title: Auto-dream cycle — autonomous consolidation + plasticity on schedule
tasks.description: Auto-sleep/auto-dream cycle — autonomous sleep-time consolidation with proper triggers. CURRENT STATE: runConsolidation exists (brain-lifecycle.ts:606) and is wired to handleSessionEndConsolidation (session-hooks.ts:129). OWNER FEEDBACK: SessionEnd is NOT the right trigger — too eager, fires on every session close. ACTUAL REQUIREMENTS: (1) Scheduled trigger (cron-like, e.g. every N hours OR daily at quiet time), (2) Idle detection (N minutes of no activity), (3) Volume threshold (after M new observations), (4) Owner-configurable thresholds via cleo config, (5) Option to DISABLE auto-trigger in favor of manual cleo memory consolidate. NOT: fire on every SessionEnd. Also evaluate: should the SessionEnd consolidation be removed or kept as a backstop? Owner to decide.
acceptance: cleo memory dream command exists
Auto-fires on session.end
Configurable schedule via cleo config
Dream log stored in BRAIN
Provider-agnostic (no Claude Code dependency)

tasks.title: cleo orchestrate plan <epicId> — return Lead/Worker spawn plan with waves

Returns {waves:[{wave:N, lead:{taskId,persona},workers:[{taskId,persona,tier,atomicScope}]}]} so the main orchestrator has a machine-readable decomposition instead of hand-rolling it. Output MUST be LAFS-compliant and deterministic for the same input.

tasks.title: Playbook runtime state machine — runtime.ts executor with iteration caps + HITL gates
tasks.description: Implement the full state machine runtime for .cantbook playbooks. The foundation (schema/parser/state/policy/approval) shipped in v2026.4.87; runtime.ts is the keystone that ties them together and makes playbooks executable. State machine: load parsed playbook → persist run in playbook_runs → advance node-by-node → on agent node call composeSpawnPayload+dispatch → on validate node run validator → on decide node branch → on approval node pause with resumeToken → on end mark complete. Iteration cap per node. Context carries state between nodes.

tasks.title: MASTER: Sentient Self-Healing Orchestrator — 4-pillar integration anchor
tasks.description: OWNER VISION 2026-04-21 (session ses_20260421173407_ac5c20): Transform CLEO from tactical task-executor into sentient self-healing self-improving orchestration substrate. Maps user's 4 architectural pillars to concrete CLEO work. Not a new coding epic — an ANCHOR that references all component work across T1075 Honcho T1106 living brain T942 sentient architecture. See .cleo/agent-outputs/T1075-honcho-integration-plan/PLAN.md for decomposition. Pillars: (1) Event-driven nervous system — durable retry-able steps where every LLM tool call is wrapped as independent step; failures trigger reflection not cascade; audit trail inherent because actions are event-sourced. Anchors T1145 deriver queue T1147 reconciler scheduler new step-level retry wrapper. (2) Hierarchical pluggable memory — .cleo/ project brain as short-term with context pruning NEXUS global as long-term with tree-structured sessions memory extracted OUT of LLM into CLEO harness. Anchors T1090 multi-pass retrieval T1146 Wave 6.3 brain_memory_trees T1089 session_narrative. (3) Sub-agent spawning with context isolation — delegate_task spawns specialists with own isolated context returning clean summaries to Core. Anchors T1140 worktree-by-default spawn SUBSTRATE T1149 Conduit A2A SUBSTRATE Wave 0.2 packages/agents cleanup BLOCKER cleo-db-lead E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND today. (4) Aggressive extensibility — model-agnostic LLM routing pluggable filesystem/sandbox backends tools/commands/skills as modular packages. Anchors Honcho llm/ layer adoption T1107 SDK parity 14 Living Brain verbs MCP/TUI surface expansion. START Pillar 1 before Pillar 2 event-driven is prerequisite for reliable hierarchical memory writes. Dogfood on v2026.4.107+.
acceptance: Pillar 1 evidence T1145 deriver queue shipped + step-level retry wrapper wraps all LLM+tool calls
Pillar 2 evidence T1090 multi-pass + T1146 trees + T1089 narrative shipped with E2E test
Pillar 3 evidence T1140 worktree-default + T1149 Conduit A2A + Wave 0.2 agent registry fix shipped + E2E test of two concurrent Leads coordinating via Conduit
Pillar 4 evidence packages/contracts SDK surface covers all 4 pillars with typed Params/Result; at least ONE non-CLI consumer (MCP or TUI) exercises the surface
Release v2026.5.x containing all 4 pillars tagged CLEO Sentient v1 with CHANGELOG milestone entry
Owner sign-off that next agent installing v2026.5.x on greenfield project inherits meaningfully more sentient substrate

tasks.title: Dispatch test regression sweep — 22 tests broken by typed-narrowing wave T1421-T1427
tasks.description:
v2026.4.142 (commit 058f363cd) bundled the T1421-T1427 typed-narrowing wave (sentient/conduit/check/nexus/tasks/admin/memory dispatch domains converted to TypedDomainHandler<T>). The wave landed 22 test failures across 6 dispatch test files that did NOT exist in v2026.4.141.

Visible patterns from sampling 2026-04-24:
- tasks.test.ts:231 — expects result.page = {mode:'none'}, got undefined (handler dropped pagination metadata)
- nexus.test.ts NexusHandler.getSupportedOperations — array content drift  
- nexus.test.ts query.list — extra field present on actual (3 keys vs 2)

Distinct from T1428 (cast-reduction hygiene) — this is contract-shape regression. Surfaced during NEXT-SESSION-HANDOFF backlog walkthrough. Sibling under T1428 in the T988 follow-on family.
acceptance: All 22 dispatch tests across 6 files pass on main without modifying behavioral assertions to mask broken handler output

Per-failure root cause classified: (a) handler dropped contract field — fix handler; (b) test asserts old shape needing update if contract genuinely changed AND change is documented; (c) handler leaks internal fields — narrow typed wrapper return shape

nexus.test.ts NexusHandler.getSupportedOperations array content matches spec
nexus.test.ts query.list response shape narrowed to contract
Repo-wide pnpm exec vitest run shows 0 failures across 6 files; biome ci . exit 0; pnpm run build exit 0
Add regression-prevention CI gate so typed-narrowing-style refactors run targeted dispatch tests pre-merge

tasks.title: T1449-PROOF: session domain — Core API alignment with Contracts (proof of concept)
tasks.description:
PROOF-OF-CONCEPT for T1449. Session is smallest dispatch domain (~670 LOC), well-bounded (start/end/status/list/resume), few internal callers. Establish the migration pattern here, then parallelize for 8 remaining domains.

Pattern to establish:
1. Read packages/contracts/src/operations/session.ts — that's the SSoT.
2. For each Core fn in packages/core/src/sessions/ that backs a dispatch op: refactor signature to (projectRoot: string, params: <Op>Params): Promise<<Op>Result>. Body destructures params.fieldX rather than positional args.
3. Update internal Core callers (other Core fns, scripts, tests) — they pass a single object instead of positional args.
4. If field names diverge (Core 'foo' vs Contract 'fooId'), rename Core field + update body + update callers.
5. In dispatch session.ts: import Core fns directly, build coreOps record, type SessionOps = OpsFromCore<typeof coreOps>, replace handler bodies with 1-3 lines (just call the Core fn with params).
6. Verify all gates green.

This is the pattern. After it lands, 8 parallel workers do the same for admin/check/conduit/nexus/pipeline/playbook/sentient/tasks.
acceptance: Every Core function in packages/core/src/sessions/ has signature (projectRoot, params: <Op>Params): Promise<<Op>Result> using SessionStartParams/SessionEndParams/etc. directly from @cleocode/contracts
All internal Core callers updated to pass single param object (no positional args)
packages/cleo/src/dispatch/domains/session.ts becomes thin pass-through: coreOps imports from sessions/* directly, type SessionOps = OpsFromCore<typeof coreOps>, dispatch handlers are 1-3 lines
Field-name divergence resolved: where session Core internal names differ from contract wire format, contract wins; Core renames + updates all internal callers
Repo-wide tsc -b exit 0; biome ci . exit 0; pnpm run build exit 0; all session domain tests pass; no regressions in adjacent domains

tasks.title: fix(orchestrate): worktree leak — auto-cleanup on cleo complete + add 'cleo orchestrate prune'
tasks.description: cleo orchestrate spawn locks each worktree under ~/.local/share/cleo/worktrees/<projectHash>/<taskId>/ with reason 'cleo-agent-T###' and never unlocks/removes it on cleo complete <id>. Result on this machine 2026-04-25: 85 worktrees accumulated (69 done/archived, 16 pending). git worktree list reported all locked; manual cleanup required 'git worktree remove -f -f' plus 'git branch -D'. Lock without cleanup is the root cause. Fix scope: (1) cleanup hook on cleo complete <id> that unlocks+removes worktree and deletes task/T### branch after verifying cherry-pick to main succeeded; (2) new 'cleo orchestrate prune' subcommand for retroactive cleanup of done/archived/cancelled worktrees; (3) lock semantics tightened so the lock is released only after cherry-pick verification, not before. See AGENTS.md ADR-055/T1140 for the worktree-by-default spec.
acceptance: cleo complete <id> auto-removes the worktree + task/T### branch when cherry-pick to main verified
cleo orchestrate prune subcommand exists and removes worktrees for tasks with status in {done, archived, cancelled}
Worktree lock 'cleo-agent-T###' is released only after cherry-pick verification succeeds — not on spawn-end
Pending tasks (status not in {done, archived, cancelled}) are NEVER removed by prune
Tests cover clean-complete path, complete-with-failed-cherry-pick (worktree retained), prune with mixed task statuses

tasks.title: T-LW-W8: HOTFIX — daemon crash-loop bugs (Studio path, node spawn, install import)
tasks.description: v2026.5.2 daemon installed via systemctl --user enable but immediately crash-loops. Three real bugs found in production: (1) Studio supervision tries to spawn ~/.npm-global/.../packages/studio/build/index.js but Studio is NOT bundled with @cleocode/cleo. FIX: make @cleocode/studio PUBLISHABLE (private: false in package.json), add as dependency of @cleocode/cleo, use import.meta.resolve('@cleocode/studio') to find Studio's bin entrypoint at runtime — never hardcode workspace paths. (2) Daemon spawns child node process at hardcoded fnm-resolved path /home/<user>/.local/share/fnm/node-versions/v24.13.1/installation/bin/node which doesn't exist in systemd's PATH context. FIX: use process.execPath (already-running node binary). (3) Runtime 'cleo daemon install' fails with 'Cannot find module @cleocode/scripts/install-daemon-service.mjs' — bundled CLI imports the install script via wrong package path. FIX: resolve relative to cleo's own package root using fileURLToPath(import.meta.url). MANDATORY end-to-end verification AFTER fix (no theater): systemctl --user enable --now cleo-daemon; sleep 60; systemctl --user status cleo-daemon | grep 'Active: active (running)' MUST match; tail log file MUST show at least one sentient tick line; cleo daemon status MUST report Studio supervised running. Provide systemctl status output + log tail in manifest as evidence.
acceptance: Daemon starts cleanly when Studio package not installed (graceful degrade to 'studio: not-available')
Node spawn uses process.execPath or PATH-resolved 'node' (not hardcoded fnm path)
cleo daemon install runtime command resolves install-daemon-service.mjs from cleo package's own scripts/ dir
systemctl --user status cleo-daemon shows 'active (running)' for at least 60 seconds without restart
Daemon log file shows at least one successful sentient tick

tasks.title: Fix CI failures — cliOutput console.log hijack + FORCE_COLOR snapshot drift
tasks.description: Three surgical fixes to make GitHub Actions CI green. ROOT CAUSES (already diagnosed): (1) cliOutput at packages/cleo/src/cli/renderers/index.ts line 374 uses console.log(envelopeString); cliError line 464 uses console.log(JSON.stringify(envelope)); also lines 261, 279. Vitest hijacks console.log for its own reporter so test mocks of process.stdout.write never see the output, breaking 17 nexus-projects-clean tests. FIX: replace console.log(s) with process.stdout.write(s + '\\n') in those 4 sites. (2) Brain-renderers snapshot tests fail in CI because GitHub Actions runners set FORCE_COLOR=1 — verified locally that FORCE_COLOR=1 produces 7 mismatches matching CI exactly. FIX: add 'env: { NO_COLOR: "1", FORCE_COLOR: "0" }' to test config in root vitest.config.ts. (3) mental-model + pipeline-stage tests are isolation flakes — quarantine with it.skip + file followup task if not trivially fixable in 30min.
acceptance: Replace console.log with process.stdout.write at lines 261, 279, 374, 464 of packages/cleo/src/cli/renderers/index.ts (and console.error at line 427 if any tests check stderr)
Add env: { NO_COLOR: '1', FORCE_COLOR: '0' } to test block in vitest.config.ts (root)
nexus-projects-clean.test.ts: 17/17 pass via pnpm --filter @cleocode/cleo test src/cli/__tests__/nexus-projects-clean -- --run
brain-renderers.test.ts: 32/32 pass with FORCE_COLOR=1 simulating CI: FORCE_COLOR=1 pnpm vitest run packages/cleo/src/cli/renderers/__tests__/brain-renderers.test.ts

tasks.title: RCASD: ADR-068 + spec — canonical agent system, supersede ADR-055 D032/D035
tasks.description: RCASD planning task for Phase 1 of the agent + playbook canonicalization. Output is ADR-068 (canonical agent system v2) and a formal spec defining: tier resolution, naming convention, install validator contract, classifier registry sync, universal-tier fallback wiring, and symmetric playbook tier resolution. ADR explicitly supersedes ADR-055 D032 and D035. Frame the architecture using the Distributed OS metaphor — Playbook is the kernel, Agents are processes, the orchestration pipeline manages them as compute resources. The canonical variable resolver order (step ⊳ playbook ⊳ session ⊳ project ⊳ env ⊳ default) is the magic that makes mustache injection seamless to end users — explicit shadowing rules MUST be in spec.
acceptance: ADR-068 written under .cleo/adrs/ with full RFC 2119 spec
Frame: CLEO as Distributed OS for Agents (Playbook=kernel, Agents=processes)
Canonical agent naming: filename basename MUST equal declared name; project-<role> for workers; cleo-subagent for universal; bare names for meta-agents
Single source-of-truth: @cleocode/agents/templates/ (delete seed-agents/ and starter-bundle/)
Symmetric playbook tier resolver: project/global/packaged tiers; no synthesis tier in Phase 1
Variable resolver canonical order documented: step bindings ⊳ playbook bindings ⊳ session ⊳ project-context ⊳ env ⊳ default
Universal-tier fallback (D035) wired into spawn validator pre-flight (Bug 6)
Decomposition into 12 child tasks with explicit acceptance criteria + dependency graph
Supersedes ADR-055 D032 (single layout) and D035 (universal-tier-only-as-resolver-fallback)

tasks.title: Schema-version sentinel for ensureConduitDb / ensureGlobalSignaldockDb
tasks.description: Implement: at the top of ensureConduitDb (after the singleton-already-open check), read _conduit_meta.schema_version. If equal to CONDUIT_SCHEMA_VERSION code constant AND _conduit_meta has a fresh row (not stale), skip applyConduitSchema and runConduitMigrations entirely — just open the DB, apply pragmas, and return. Same for ensureGlobalSignaldockDb using its corresponding sentinel. The fall-through path (sentinel missing or mismatched) still runs the full DDL + migration replay so fresh DBs and version bumps work correctly.

tasks.title: Fix scaffold.ts cleanEnv git identity fallback
tasks.description: In packages/core/src/scaffold.ts lines 898-914, the code uses cleanEnv (which strips HOME and other env vars) when calling git config user.email to CHECK if an identity exists. Because HOME is stripped, git cannot find ~/.gitconfig, so the read fails and the fallback writes cleo@local/CLEO to the project's local .git/config even when the user has a valid global identity. Fix: use process.env (or at minimum preserve HOME) when reading git config to check existence, so the fallback only fires on systems that truly have no git identity configured.
acceptance: Read check uses real env with HOME preserved
Fallback only writes when no global or local identity exists
No regression in CI/container path where global config truly absent

tasks.title: CANT AST → Pi tool/protocol registration bridge
tasks.description: Pi extension bridge at $CLEO_HOME/pi-extensions/cant-loader.ts that takes the AST from loadCantFile (T274) and maps each Section and Statement onto Pi's extension API. Consumes WorkflowExecutor from @cleocode/core (the cant/ namespace) which must be fully functional via T287 (epic) — today WorkflowExecutor is dead code with 3 missing statement handlers (Expression/Property/PipeStep, closed by T289), 3 stubs (Session/Pipeline/Directive, closed by T290/T291/T292), and a broken condition evaluator (closed by T288). This task implements the mapping layer only; execution correctness is the responsibility of T287's subtasks. Mappings: (1) AgentDef → pi.registerTool with tool name from agent.name, description from agent.properties[description], parameter schema from agent.permissions + properties. (2) ProtocolDef / protocol constraints in AgentDef → pi.on('before_tool_call', validator) that blocks non-conforming calls with the protocol's error message. (3) HookDef → pi.on(canonicalEvent, handler) matching the 31 canonical CAAMP events (16 provider + 15 domain) from crates/cant-core validation rules S06/H01. (4) WorkflowDef → pi.registerCommand('/workflow <name>', async (args) => executor.execute(workflowAst, ctx)). All 16 statement types inside workflow bodies (Binding, Expression, Directive, Property, Comment, Session, Parallel, Conditional, Repeat, ForLoop, LoopUntil, TryCatch, ApprovalGate, Pipeline, PipeStep, Output) must round-trip correctly via WorkflowExecutor. (5) PipelineDef → pi.registerCommand('/pipeline <name>') that calls WorkflowExecutor which delegates to cant_execute_pipeline napi binding for the deterministic subprocess work. (6) SkillDef → Pi skill metadata registered via pi.ui or settings (Pi has no first-class skill registration API — map to prompts/). (7) ProseBlock content in any property → rendered verbatim in system prompt, preserving indentation per T07 validation rule. (8) LetBinding at top level → session-scoped variable registered via bridge runtime context. The extension uses pi.on('session_start', attach) and pi.on('session_shutdown', detach) to manage lifecycle; on detach, all registered tools/commands/handlers are idempotently unregistered.
acceptance: Pi extension at $CLEO_HOME/pi-extensions/cant-loader.ts compiles as a valid Pi extension TS file with export default function that takes pi: ExtensionAPI
AgentDef sections register as Pi tools via pi.registerTool with name, description, parameter schemas, and permissions mapped correctly
Protocol constraints register as before_tool_call validators that block non-conforming calls with the protocol's error message
HookDef sections register against canonical CAAMP events (all 31 validated against crates/cant-core S06/H01 rules)
WorkflowDef sections register as /workflow <name> slash commands; invoking them calls WorkflowExecutor.execute() and all 16 statement types round-trip correctly (depends on T280)
PipelineDef sections register as /pipeline <name> commands that invoke WorkflowExecutor which delegates to cant_execute_pipeline napi
ProseBlock content preserves multi-line indentation when rendered in system prompt
Multiple .cant files in the cant/ directory all register without name conflict; conflicts across files produce a clear error with both source paths

Integration test uses cleo-subagent.cant fixture: installs the extension, starts a Pi session, verifies all expected tools/hooks/commands are visible, and executes at least one workflow through WorkflowExecutor end-to-end
Unregister on session_shutdown is idempotent and complete (no leaked tools/hooks after tear-down)
Extension file is importable via @cleocode/caamp/extensions/cant-loader so the same entry point can be tested in isolation from a running Pi instance
notes: 2026-04-07 16:53:58 UTC: 2026-04-07 16:00: Audit-driven rewrite. T275 is the Pi extension bridge only; execution correctness of the 16 statement types lives in T280 which must complete first. The Pi extension and cleo agent start share the same @cleocode/cant + @cleocode/core imports — no shared loader module to extract.

tasks.title: CANT persona wiring — classify(task) → .cant → spawn payload {tools,model,skills}
tasks.description: Wire .cant persona files into the spawn path via the AGENT REGISTRY (SSoT), not fs-scan. packages/cant/src/composer.ts already has composeSpawnPayload + escalateTier but the cleo orchestrate spawn path (packages/core/src/orchestration/spawn-prompt.ts shipped in T882) does NOT call it AND does NOT consult packages/core/src/store/agent-registry-accessor.ts. Fix: classify(task) returns {agentId, role, confidence} by querying the registry with task context (labels, type, size). Composer then loads the registered .cant file via the path stored in the registry row (not fs-scan). Registry row carries {agentId, tier, cantPath, tools[], model, skills[], classification}. Confidence floor 0.5 per T377 memory. Ensures cleo agent attach/detach/remove actually affects spawn. Pairs with T898 (registry-backed resolution), T899 (tier precedence), T900 (install/attach wiring).
acceptance: classify(task) returns {role, persona, confidence} for all 5 default personas (cleo-prime, dev-lead, rust-lead, db-lead, historian)
composeSpawnPayload is reached from cleo orchestrate spawn (single code path)
spawn payload includes {prompt, persona, model, tools[], skills[]} — verify via JSON output
.cant files resolved from .cleo/agents/ first, then packages/agents/ fallback
confidence < 0.5 falls back to generic cleo-subagent with warning in meta
tests: 5 persona resolutions + 1 fallback + 1 missing-persona error case

tasks.title: BRAIN auto-reconcile: semantic conflict detection + auto-supersession of decisions
tasks.description: Owner-flagged 2026-04-21T16:00Z: this-session demonstrated D019/D021/D022 supersession is prose-only — no structural link between decisions. Auto-reconcile is the missing half of T-BRAIN-LIVING (Hebbian plasticity was step 1 usage-weighting; semantic conflict resolution is step 2). Without this cleo memory decision-find returns contradictory active rules to future-self agents. Human brains consolidate during sleep iterating on conflicting signals — cleo should too.
acceptance: New brain_decisions column supersedes_json (array of superseded decision IDs) + superseded_by_id (back-pointer)
decision-store detects semantic overlap with existing via embedding similarity + keyword match; if >0.85 similarity + contradictory keyword signals flag conflict
On conflict: return interactive prompt 'D<new> appears to conflict with D<old> on topic X — supersede (S) coexist (C) abort (A)'
Non-interactive mode: auto-flag + write to pending-reconciliation queue for HITL review at session start
Session briefing surfaces unreconciled conflicts at top of output
Dream-cycle (brain-lifecycle.ts) gains a consolidation pass: reads recent decisions find topical clusters propose merge/supersede relationships
cleo memory decision-find filters out superseded by default unless --include-superseded
Retroactive audit: walk existing decisions detect D019→D021→D022 supersession chain from prose references write to new column
Test: synthetic DB with 3 conflicting decisions run reconcile assert exactly 1 active 2 superseded
biome + build + test green
Worktree+PR per D022

tasks.title: Wave 0.2: packages/agents/ cleanup + PeerIdentity contract + E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND regression hardening
tasks.description: Lead B work. Per other-agent 2026-04-22 delta: spawn is now operational (E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND no longer blocks) but canonicalize the packages/agents/ structure before the rest of the PSYCHE work lands. Per ADR-055 (v2026.4.110+), packages/agents/ ships exactly three surfaces: (1) cleo-subagent.cant universal protocol base at package root, (2) seed-agents/ with 4 generic role templates (orchestrator-generic, dev-lead-generic, code-worker-generic, docs-worker-generic), (3) meta/agent-architect.cant meta-agent. Three deliverables: (a) consolidate packages/agents/cleo-subagent/cleo-subagent.cant ↔ packages/agents/seed-agents/cleo-subagent.cant to ONE canonical path (remove AGENT.md standalone doc — fold into .cant), (b) define PeerIdentity type in packages/contracts/src/peer.ts (peerId peerKind cantFile displayName description), (c) wire packages/cant/src/native-loader.ts to produce PeerIdentity[] from seed-agents/. Regression test guards E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND against the canonical ship surface (cleo-subagent + the 4 generic templates + agent-architect). CLEO-internal dogfood personas (cleo-prime et al.) live in this repo's .cleo/cant/agents/ and are not part of the ship surface regression.

tasks.description: getProjectRoot() in packages/core/src/paths.ts walks up from cwd looking for .cleo/ and explicitly guards against $HOME and / (T889/T909). It does NOT guard against /mnt/projects (or any directory above multiple sibling project roots). On this machine 2026-04-25 a stray /mnt/projects/.cleo existed (empty schema, tasks=0 sessions=0). Any cleo command run from a non-CLEO subfolder under /mnt/projects/ would walk up and adopt that .cleo as the project root, silently writing to the wrong DBs — same orphan-DB vector ADR-037 / T889 closed for $HOME, just one level deeper. Fix scope: (1) refuse a candidate root that lacks a co-located .git/ unless explicitly opted in via CLEO_ROOT env, OR (2) require .cleo/project-info.json projectHash to match a content-derived hash of the candidate root. Either prevents the trap. (1) is simpler and aligns with ADR-013 §9 (data must be project-tier).
acceptance: getProjectRoot refuses a candidate .cleo/ that has no sibling .git/ unless CLEO_ROOT is set explicitly
Stray /mnt/projects/.cleo cannot hijack sibling projects after the fix — verified by integration test
Existing T889/T909 $HOME and / guards remain intact
Error message guides the user to run cleo init in the correct directory
Test cases cover .cleo with .git (accepted), .cleo without .git (rejected), CLEO_ROOT override (accepted regardless)

tasks.title: R2: LAFS envelope use-case analysis — LAFSEnvelope vs CliEnvelope, recommend keep-both / collapse-to-SSoT / hybrid
tasks.description: Pre-Wave-1 research. Audit B identified two envelope shapes: full LAFSEnvelope ($schema, _meta, result, success — used internally by @cleocode/lafs SDK) and CliEnvelope (success, data, error, meta — wire format on stdout, canonical per ADR-039). Both are legit per ADR-039 but the conversion path silently drops fields. Owner directive: 'Review both LAFSEnvelope + CliEnvelope (legit per ADR-039) then determine use case and if need to collapse into SSoT'. Investigate: (1) What ADR-039 actually mandates as canonical; (2) Whether two shapes serve genuinely different purposes or are accidental duplication; (3) Whether downstream SDK consumers rely on the full LAFSEnvelope shape; (4) Recommend boundary or unification. No code changes — research only.
acceptance: Document exact shapes of LAFSEnvelope (full SDK protocol) AND CliEnvelope (wire format) with field-by-field comparison
Document where each is used today — every importer/consumer per envelope type
Document the ADR-039 rationale for the dual structure
Document the conversion pathways between them and which (if any) lose data

Recommendation: KEEP DUAL (with clearer boundary docs) / COLLAPSE TO ONE (which one becomes canonical) / HYBRID (e.g. CliEnvelope is canonical wire format, LAFSEnvelope is internal-only) — with rationale
Output: structured research report at .cleo/agent-outputs/ATC-2026-05-01-contracts-refactor/RESEARCH-R2-lafs-envelope.md
Owner can read report and approve recommendation before W1 LAFS unification implementation

tasks.title: ADR: CLEO coordination layers (workflow / messaging / storage)
tasks.description: Write ADR formalizing the layering: Workflow (harness/orchestrate) → Messaging (Conduit) → Storage (DataAccessors) → Data (DBs). Each layer may depend only on layers below; reverse dependencies forbidden. Explicitly states: harness MAY use Conduit, Conduit MUST NOT know about harness. This prevents recurring graph-AI false-positive flags about 'overlapping coordination systems' and gives contributors a mental model that explains why there are multiple feedback channels (Conduit messages + agent-output file drops + completion gates + BRAIN observations) — they serve different layers and are not redundant.
acceptance: ADR specifies the 5 layers with explicit dependency direction
Each existing feedback channel (Conduit, .cleo/agent-outputs/*.md, completion gates, BRAIN observations) is mapped to a specific layer with rationale
ADR is referenced from the harness and Conduit module headers
gitnexus query for 'three-tier' or 'overlap' returns the ADR as canonical answer

tasks.title: MCP subprocess lifecycle + crash recovery
tasks.description: Manages the MCP server subprocess lifecycle inside the generated Pi extension. Spawns on first tool call (lazy), kills on Pi session shutdown hook, auto-restarts with exponential backoff if the subprocess exits unexpectedly mid-session. Shares a single subprocess across all tool calls in a session (not one per call). Tracks process state: idle, initializing, ready, crashed, shutdown.
acceptance: First tool call spawns the subprocess lazily
Subsequent tool calls reuse the same subprocess
session_shutdown hook kills the subprocess cleanly (SIGTERM then SIGKILL after 5s)
Mid-session crash triggers restart with backoff (100ms, 500ms, 2s) up to 3 retries
Restart re-runs the initialize handshake before serving the next tool call
Unit tests kill the subprocess and verify recovery

tasks.title: caamp pi cant install/remove/list verbs
tasks.description: CLI verbs for the CANT bridge, honouring the three-tier scope hierarchy (project/user/global) from ADR-035 D1. Verbs: (1) 'caamp pi cant install <source> [--scope project|user|global]' — fetches a .cant file via the existing source fetcher (npm/git/http/local), validates via @cleocode/cant loadCantFile (T274) which runs all 42 cant-core validation rules, and copies to the target tier: project=<cwd>/.pi/cant/, user=$PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR/cant/ or ~/.pi/agent/cant/, global=$CLEO_HOME/pi-extensions/cant/. Conflict-on-write errors unless --force (per universal rule in ADR-035 cross-cutting concerns). (2) 'caamp pi cant remove <name> [--scope ...]' — idempotent delete, tolerates ENOENT. (3) 'caamp pi cant list [--scope ...]' — reads across all 3 tiers, deduplicates by name with higher-precedence tier winning, reports tier per entry, shows agent/workflow/pipeline/hook counts per file parsed from the AST. (4) 'caamp pi cant validate <path>' — runs loadCantFile against an arbitrary file and reports errors + warnings (42 rules) without installing anything. All verbs use LAFS envelope output shape matching other caamp commands. Pi-absent fallback: if Pi is not installed, exit status 4 with message 'Pi is not installed. Run: caamp providers install pi'.
acceptance: install validates via loadCantFile (42 rules) before copying; invalid files rejected with the cant-core rule ID + message
install errors on existing target unless --force; --scope selects project
user
global tier per ADR-035 D1
remove is idempotent and tolerates ENOENT
list reads across all 3 tiers, dedupes by name with precedence, reports tier per entry
list shows agent/workflow/pipeline/hook counts per .cant file from parsed AST
validate verb runs loadCantFile and reports errors + warnings without installing
Unit tests use tmp dirs and real fixture .cant files from crates/cant-core/fixtures/ and .cleo/agents/
LAFS envelope output shape matches other caamp commands (success/error/data fields)
Pi-absent fallback: exit status 4 with E_NOT_FOUND message when pi binary missing
notes: 2026-04-07 16:54:31 UTC: 2026-04-07 16:05: Added validate verb; explicit three-tier scope handling per ADR-035 D1 cross-cutting. Conflict semantics match other caamp install verbs.

tasks.title: Implement path-ACL enforcement in cleo-cant-bridge.ts tool_call hook
tasks.description: Extend the tool_call hook in packages/cleo-os/extensions/cleo-cant-bridge.ts to enforce path permissions. When a tool call is Edit, Write, or Bash (with a file argument), extract the target file path and test it against the agent's declared permissions.paths.write globs using micromatch. If the path does not match any write glob, reject the call with a LAFS error envelope (code: E_FORBIDDEN, fix_hint: 'Check permissions.paths.write in your .cant definition'). Allow calls when no ACL is declared.

When agent has no permissions.paths block, all paths are allowed (fail-open)

tasks.title: Auto-tier selection — orchestrator=2, lead=1, worker=0 with size/type heuristics
tasks.description: Replace manual --tier N flag with auto-selection. Algorithm: role=orchestrator→tier 2, role=lead→tier 1, role=worker→tier 0. Override rules: task.size=large OR task.type=epic bumps +1 tier; task has research/spec labels bumps +1; presence of --tier N flag overrides auto. Rationale: orchestrator needs full protocol + skill excerpts to make routing decisions; leads need the protocol but not skill excerpts; workers need just the task and return contract (protocol is inherited from AGENTS.md harness load). Manual flag still wins for explicit overrides.

override: labels includes research

Paths: packages/agents/seed-agents/*.cant (7 files: cleo-db-lead cleo-dev cleo-historian cleo-prime cleo-rust-lead cleo-subagent cleoos-opus-orchestrator) AND .cleo/cant/agents/*.cant (cleo-orchestrator code-worker dev-lead docs-worker AND the copies that got re-installed from seed-agents). Also: .cleo/agents/*.{cant,md} (legacy path with mix). For each agent file classify: (a) GENERIC-TEMPLATE (suitable for shipping as starter), (b) CLEO-PROJECT-SPECIFIC (mentions CleoCode/SignalDock/cleo crates/specific lore), (c) UNIVERSAL-PROTOCOL-BASE (cleo-subagent.cant). Design the final content for packages/agents/seed-agents/ based on classification: (1) cleo-subagent.cant stays (universal base), (2) promote/refine generic templates from .cleo/cant/agents/ (cleo-orchestrator→orchestrator-generic etc with variable placeholders per R2), (3) relocate cleo-specific ones to .cleo/cant/agents/ only. Write to .cleo/agent-outputs/T-AGENTS-PRE-WAVE/R3-CONTENT-AUDIT.md with per-file classification table + proposed final tree.
acceptance: R3-CONTENT-AUDIT.md written at .cleo/agent-outputs/T-AGENTS-PRE-WAVE/
Classification table with every .cant file from all three paths (seed-agents/ cleo/cant/agents/ cleo/agents/) labeled GENERIC-TEMPLATE CLEO-SPECIFIC or UNIVERSAL
Proposed final packages/agents/seed-agents/ tree listing (name + purpose + why generic)
Proposed final .cleo/cant/agents/ tree listing cleo-project personas
Proposed deprecation path for .cleo/agents/ legacy files
Variables each generic template should expose (matching R2 syntax)
3+ example template bodies drafted with placeholders

tasks.title: T1252-followup: wire orchestrate-engine to pass ConduitSubscriptionConfig
tasks.description: Followup to T1252 discovered during v2026.4.118 Phase B orchestration. T1252 shipped ConduitSubscriptionConfig interface + buildConduitSubscriptionBlock + tier-1/2 gating in packages/core/src/orchestration/spawn-prompt.ts, but no caller passes the config. cleo orchestrate spawn at tier 1 still omits the ## CONDUIT Subscription section. Fix: in orchestrate-engine derive wave topic epic-X.wave-Y and coordination topic epic-X.coordination from task.parentId and task.id then pass as conduitSubscription to buildSpawnPromptInput. Tier-0 intentionally skips the section per T1252 design; tier-0 Leads need CONDUIT directive in orchestrator header or get lifted to tier-1 for auto-inject. Blocks practical A2A dogfood in Lead dispatch flow.
acceptance: orchestrate-engine derives topic names and passes conduitSubscription
spawn-prompt test verifies section appears on tier 1+ with config
manual smoke test passes
biome build test green

tasks.title: T-ENGINE-MIGRATION: Migrate 15,297 LOC of CLI engine logic to @cleocode/core (real T-THIN-WRAPPER)
tasks.description: The TRUE T-THIN-WRAPPER goal that predecessor falsely claimed complete. 17 engines in packages/cleo/src/dispatch/engines/ contain business logic that violates ADR-057/058. All logic must move to @cleocode/core with logical domain file separation. CLI dispatch engines should have ZERO logic — only thin shims if any. Operator strategy: highest-leverage first = task-engine.ts (2233 LOC) first. Subsequent: nexus-engine (2016), orchestrate-engine (1962), system-engine (1855), release-engine (1517), session-engine (1299), validate-engine (1245), tools-engine (878), then smaller ones. Per-engine recipe: extract pure functions to core/<domain>/, leave thin shims, update domain handlers to call core directly, run full test suite, verify+complete.
acceptance: All 17 engines reduced to <100 LOC of pure CLI-shim code each
All business logic in @cleocode/core organized by domain
No function duplication across core domains
Each domain handler in packages/cleo/src/dispatch/domains/<X>.ts handler body ≤5 LOC
pnpm run build clean for full graph
pnpm run test 100% pass
biome + tsc clean

tasks.title: T-LW-W3: Tiered hygiene scan — filesystem → SQL → Jaccard → LLM
tasks.description: Upgrade T1636's hygiene-scan.ts to tier-escalate. The 4 existing scans (orphan, top-level, content, premature-close-leak) currently emit BRAIN observations from raw SQL findings. Add a new escalation step: when a finding is AMBIGUOUS (e.g., orphan task whose parent is 'done' but task has recent updatedAt — could be legitimately in-progress, could be abandoned), call the daemon-provider LLM with structured-output schema {is_real_defect: boolean, confidence: 0..1, recommended_action: 'auto-fix'|'propose'|'ignore', reasoning: string}. Auto-execute high-confidence non-destructive fixes; emit Tier-2 sentient proposal for medium confidence; ignore for confident-not-real. Use central credentials.ts from W1 (depends on W1). Cost discipline: track LLM call count per scan in stats; default budget cap 50 calls/dream cycle.
acceptance: hygiene-scan.ts has tier-escalation: filesystem/SQL/Jaccard always run; LLM only on ambiguous findings
LLM call returns structured output {is_real_defect, confidence, recommended_action, reasoning}
High-confidence (>=0.9) non-destructive findings auto-execute (e.g., re-parent unambiguous orphan)
Medium confidence (0.7-0.9) emits Tier-2 proposal
Low confidence (<0.7) marked HITL-required
Per-scan LLM call budget configurable (default 50); cap exceeded -> log warning + skip remaining
10+ new tests cover deterministic-decided path (no LLM), ambiguous-decided path (LLM call), budget cap, structured-output parsing

tasks.title: Repository pattern: per-DB DataAccessor interfaces in contracts
tasks.description: ARCHITECTURE: Repurpose the existing DataAccessor interface as an UMBRELLA covering all DBs, not as a tasks-only interface. Today @cleocode/contracts/data-accessor.ts defines DataAccessor with tasks-domain methods (loadArchive, queryTasks, upsertTask, getSession). Expand to a sub-accessor map: interface DataAccessor { tasks: TaskAccessor; brain: BrainAccessor; conduit: ConduitAccessor; nexus: NexusAccessor; signaldock: SignaldockAccessor; telemetry: TelemetryWriter; docs: DocsAccessor }. The CURRENT DataAccessor methods move into TaskAccessor (the existing 96 call sites change from getAccessor().queryTasks(...) to getAccessor().tasks.queryTasks(...) — mechanical migration). Each sub-accessor is independently mockable for tests, capability-typed for consumers (function f(da: Pick<DataAccessor, 'tasks'|'brain'>) declares exactly which DBs it touches), and engine-neutral so the future PostgresDataAccessor (T9062 cloud-sync epic) is a drop-in replacement. DocsAccessor wraps the llmtxt SDK + manifest.db (defined in sibling task T9063). This pattern matches Rust signaldock-storage::traits and the Repository pattern from enterprise architecture.
acceptance: DataAccessor interface in @cleocode/contracts repurposed as umbrella with sub-accessor properties (tasks, brain, conduit, nexus, signaldock, telemetry, docs) — current task-domain methods move to TaskAccessor sub-interface
All 6 sub-accessors implemented in @cleocode/core wrapping the corresponding DBs (sqlite-backed today, engine-neutral by design)
Mechanical migration of 96 non-test call sites from getAccessor().X() to getAccessor().tasks.X() — codemod scripted, not hand-edited
Capability-typing demonstrated: at least one consumer function declares (da: Pick<DataAccessor, 'tasks'
'brain'>) and is unit-tested with a partial mock
Test suite has in-memory mock implementations for all 6 sub-accessors
getAccessor renamed to getDataAccessor (singular semantics — returns the umbrella) with deprecation re-export
Forward-compat for T9062: the umbrella interface accepts a PostgresDataAccessor implementation as a drop-in

tasks.title: Brain migrations: add statement-breakpoints to 7 multi-statement migration.sql files
tasks.description:
Fix the 7 brain migrations whose multiple SQL statements were silently truncated to first-statement-only by node:sqlite prepare(). FILES (file:line evidence from 2026-05-07 investigation):

1. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260424000001_t1260-provenance-class/migration.sql — 4 ALTER TABLE statements lines 30,33,36,39
2. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260424000002_t1145-add-deriver-queue/migration.sql — CREATE TABLE + 3 CREATE INDEX (lines 13-34)
3. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260424000003_t1145-extend-brain-observations/migration.sql — 4 ALTER + 2 CREATE INDEX (lines 12-21)
4. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260424000004_t1146-add-brain-memory-trees/migration.sql — CREATE TABLE + 2 indexes (lines 12-23)
5. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260424000005_t1147-add-brain-v2-candidate/migration.sql — CREATE TABLE + 3 indexes (lines 23-38)
6. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260424000006_t1402-rename-staging-table/migration.sql — 7 ops: RENAME + 3 DROP INDEX + 3 CREATE INDEX (lines 23-31)
7. packages/core/migrations/drizzle-brain/20260504000001_t1826-decisions-v2/migration.sql — 7 ALTER + 1 CREATE INDEX + 1 backfill UPDATE (lines 18-50)

INSERT '--> statement-breakpoint' BETWEEN each consecutive SQL statement. DO NOT change DDL semantics. DO NOT remove ensureColumns() call sites in memory-sqlite.ts (kept as legacy-upgrade repair).
acceptance: Each of 7 named migration.sql files contains '--> statement-breakpoint' between every SQL statement
migration-lint passes (existing RULE-1 still satisfied — no trailing breakpoints)
Fresh cleo init in clean tmpdir produces zero brain.db 'Adding missing column' warnings
All existing brain migration tests still pass
All columns/indexes that were previously safety-net-added now applied via the migration journal
No regression in legacy-upgrade path: brain.db with these columns from prior safety-net repair must continue to migrate without error

tasks.title: AGENTS.md deduplication — detect harness-loaded context, skip CLEO-INJECTION embed
tasks.description: Tier 1 spawn prompts currently embed CLEO-INJECTION.md (~9KB) even though Claude Code / agent harnesses auto-load AGENTS.md → which @-refs CLEO-INJECTION.md → already in context before the spawn prompt arrives. This duplicates ~9KB per spawn. Fix: spawn payload gains harnessContextHint field (default: claude-code implies CLEO-INJECTION already loaded). When hint is set, spawn-prompt.ts emits a single-line pointer (See AGENTS.md → CLEO-INJECTION.md, already loaded) instead of the full embed. Caller (dispatch layer / cleo-subagent runtime) can override with --embed-injection to force inclusion (non-Claude-Code harnesses). Saves ~9KB × every spawn = meaningful context budget restoration.
acceptance: spawn-prompt.ts accepts {harnessContextHint:'claude-code'
'generic'
'bare'} option
claude-code hint → emit pointer line, skip CLEO-INJECTION embed
generic hint → embed (current behavior)
bare hint → embed + extra protocol scaffolding
cleo orchestrate spawn defaults to claude-code hint (the primary harness)
--embed-injection CLI flag forces embed regardless of hint
tier 1 prompt size drops from ~14KB to ~5KB when hint is claude-code
snapshot tests for all 3 hint values

tasks.title: R4: Meta-agent design + .cantbook playbook integration
tasks.description: Design meta-agent concept + verify playbook integration. Meta-agent = agent that constructs other agents by reading project context + templates + emitting customized .cant files. Scope: (1) Design agent-architect.cant — the first meta-agent — with CANT prompt defining its role, tools, inputs (project description), outputs (customized agent set written to .cleo/cant/agents/). (2) Verify integration with existing playbook system (packages/playbooks/): read packages/playbooks/starter/rcasd.cantbook + ivtr.cantbook + release.cantbook — understand how agents are referenced from playbooks. Can playbooks invoke meta-agents to synthesize project-specific agents at install time? (3) Design cleo init --install-seed-agents flow update: instead of copying static files to .cleo/cant/agents/ it should optionally invoke agent-architect meta-agent to customize templates using variable substitution. (4) Propose packages/agents/meta/ directory for all meta-agents. Write to .cleo/agent-outputs/T-AGENTS-PRE-WAVE/R4-META-AGENT-DESIGN.md.

tasks.title: T-LW-W4: Real LLM dream cycle — BRAIN observation synthesis
tasks.description: Build packages/core/src/sentient/dream-cycle.ts implementing real cognitive dreaming. The cycle (default every 4h, configurable via DREAM_CYCLE_INTERVAL_MS): (1) collect last 24h of BRAIN observations across types (hygiene:*, session:*, decision:*, pattern:*); (2) bucket by topic via embedding/clustering OR semantic Jaccard if embeddings unavailable; (3) for each cluster of 5+ related observations, call LLM with structured-output schema returning extracted {decisions, patterns, learnings, constraints} (mirroring llm-extraction.ts pattern); (4) verify-and-store extracted memories via existing extraction-gate; (5) emit dream-cycle digest to BRAIN as observation 'sentient:dream-cycle-N'. Depends on W1 (centralized credentials) — uses daemon LLM provider config. Wire into safeRunTick alongside stage-drift and hygiene-scan, but at LONGER cadence (4h vs 30min for stage-drift).
acceptance: dream-cycle.ts implements runDreamCycle + safeRunDreamCycle (mirror of stage-drift/hygiene patterns)
Collects last-24h BRAIN observations across multiple types
Buckets by topic (Jaccard primary; embeddings if available)
For 5+-observation clusters: calls LLM with structured-output schema
Extracted memories pass through existing verify-and-store gate
Dream-cycle digest stored as 'sentient:dream-cycle-<runId>' observation

tasks.title: ct-lead skill: Phase Lead orchestration playbook for spawning+supervising worker swarm
tasks.description: Author the ct-lead skill at skills/ct-lead/SKILL.md (CLEO project tier — NOT global ~/.agents) defining the Phase Lead orchestration playbook: when spawned by an Orchestrator with role=orchestrator, the Lead subscribes to epic-<TID>.wave-<n> conduit topic, spawns N workers with role=leaf via delegate_task, awaits rollupWaveStatus convergence, and returns a single summary contract string to the parent Orchestrator. Include references/spawn-pattern.md (3/5/10 worker fanout examples) and references/aggregation-protocol.md (manifest+conduit drain semantics, retry policy on partial failures, 600s subagent timeout handling). Cross-references ADR-070 (T9081), rollupWaveStatus (T9082), ct-orchestrator skill. Implements T9084 child of T9080.

tasks.title: Atomic task enforcement — worker role rejects tasks with >3 files or no file scope
tasks.description: ORC-006 (max 3 files per worker) is documented but not enforced. Add validation: when role=worker, spawn rejects tasks lacking AC.files declaration, OR where AC.files.length > 3, OR where --files count > 3. Error: E_ATOMICITY_VIOLATION with fix hint (split into subtasks, or promote to lead role). Applies only to worker role — leads and orchestrators have no file cap. Ensures workers stay truly ephemeral and reviewable.
acceptance: validateAtomicity(task, role) in packages/core/src/orchestration/atomicity.ts
worker role + AC.files missing → E_ATOMICITY_VIOLATION
worker role + AC.files.length > 3 → error with fix hint
worker role + AC.files.length ≤ 3 → pass
lead/orchestrator roles → always pass
cleo orchestrate spawn T### --role worker runs the check
CLI error message suggests cleo add … --parent T### to split
tests: worker pass/fail, lead bypass, 3-file boundary, missing AC.files

tasks.title: T-LW-W5: Tiered semantic duplicate detection (BM25 → Jaccard → LLM)
tasks.description: Upgrade T1633's BRAIN duplicate detection on cleo add to tier-escalate. Current behavior: BM25/RRF score from memory.find. Add: (1) when BM25 score is in 0.5-0.85 range (uncertain — neither clear-match nor clear-different), compute Jaccard on title+description+labels; (2) when Jaccard ALSO inconclusive (0.4-0.7), call LLM with both task descriptions and structured-output schema {are_duplicate: boolean, confidence: 0..1, distinction: string|null, suggestion: 'merge'|'keep-both'|'block-new'}; (3) bypass LLM if both BM25 and Jaccard agree on either extreme. Cost discipline: max 1 LLM call per cleo add invocation. Depends on W1 (credentials) and W3 (escalation pattern shared).
acceptance: Tiered escalation in tasks/add.ts: BM25 first; Jaccard fallback when BM25 ambiguous (0.5-0.85); LLM final tier when Jaccard also ambiguous (0.4-0.7)
LLM call returns structured output {are_duplicate, confidence, distinction, suggestion}
Per-add LLM call budget: max 1 call (cost-discipline)
--force-duplicate audit log preserved (T1633's existing behavior unchanged in normal path)
Tests cover: clear-match (BM25 only), clear-different (BM25 only), uncertain (Jaccard escalation), uncertain+uncertain (LLM escalation), force-duplicate bypass

tasks.title: Stream 6: GitNexus Code Intelligence Absorption
tasks.description: Port key GitNexus patterns into @cleocode/nexus for code intelligence. (1) Language provider strategy pattern — LanguageProvider interface with per-language extractors (type, import, call, heritage). (2) Graph data model — node types (Function, Class, Interface, Method, etc.) and relationship types (CALLS, IMPORTS, EXTENDS, IMPLEMENTS, etc.) with confidence scoring. (3) Impact analysis algorithm — BFS traversal with depth-based risk grouping (d=1 WILL BREAK, d=2 LIKELY AFFECTED, d=3 MAY NEED TESTING). (4) Persistent symbol index using Drizzle/SQLite (not LadybugDB). Adapt GitNexus 14-phase pipeline concepts for CLEO context. Do NOT port: LadybugDB, web UI, MCP server, embeddings, community detection (future).

Output includes BOTH injected-anomaly proposals AND zero-false-positive on unrelated symbols

Binary smoke against published @cleocode/cleo in /tmp

tasks.title: Wave 5: Deriver Queue Pipeline — durable background derivation worker
tasks.description: Honcho's /mnt/projects/honcho/src/deriver/ is a full producer/consumer queue with durable state retries dead-letter — NOT the setImmediate fire-and-forget pattern T1088 proposes. For production-grade dialectic evaluation replace T1088's sync wire-up with a proper queue. See PLAN.md Part 4 Wave 5. Depends on Wave 3 (T1082 dialectic-evaluator.ts must exist). Three children: (5.1) packages/core/src/memory/derivation-queue.ts — brain_derivation_queue table schema + SDK (enqueueDerivation claimNextDerivation completeDerivation failDerivation) using SQLite SKIP-LOCKED txn pattern. (5.2) packages/core/src/memory/derivation-worker.ts — worker process run via cleo memory derive-worker --watch OR spawned sub-process in sentient daemon. Retry: exp backoff max 5 attempts then DLQ. (5.3) Replace T1088 setImmediate with enqueueDerivation — non-blocking as before but DURABLE. Honcho refs: src/deriver/{enqueue consumer queue_manager prompts}.py
acceptance: packages/core/src/memory/derivation-queue.ts ships with SKIP-LOCKED claim semantics
brain_derivation_queue migration generated + backfilled
derivation-worker.ts CLI command cleo memory derive-worker --watch functional
T1088 wire-up migrated from setImmediate to enqueueDerivation call
Test: kill worker mid-derivation + restart → derivation completes without data loss
Test: 5 consecutive failures moves derivation to DLQ
biome + build + test green

tasks.description: Implementation Lead I2. See R2-VARIABLE-SYNTAX-DESIGN.md in .cleo/agent-outputs/T-AGENTS-PRE-WAVE/. Deliverables: (1) packages/contracts/src/operations/variable-substitution.ts — VariableResolver SubstitutionResult SubstitutionContext types per R2 §4. tasks.title: T-HR-S2: BRAIN-powered duplicate-task detection on cleo add
tasks.description: Build core safeguard: before creating a new task via cleo add, query BRAIN for semantically similar active tasks (title + description + labels). If similarity score >0.85 against any active task, WARN the operator with a list of candidates. If --force-duplicate is not passed and at least one match >0.92, REJECT with E_DUPLICATE_TASK_LIKELY. Use existing BRAIN observation/pattern retrieval (memory.find / observe pipeline) for similarity. Wire into packages/core/src/tasks/add.ts. This prevents the 'multiple Auth API epics' duplication issue currently visible (T1337, T1354, T1376 — three Epic Auth imported variants).

tasks.title: T-LW-W6: Daemon auto-start on cleo install (systemd / launchd)
tasks.description: When 'npm install -g @cleocode/cleo' runs, postinstall MUST register the daemon as a user-level system service so it auto-starts and persists across reboots. Linux (systemd): write ~/.config/systemd/user/cleo-daemon.service unit + run 'systemctl --user enable --now cleo-daemon'. macOS (launchd): write ~/Library/LaunchAgents/io.cleocode.daemon.plist + run 'launchctl bootstrap gui/1000' or load. Skip on Windows for now (file followup). Idempotent: re-install does not duplicate or restart unnecessarily. cleo uninstall (or 'cleo daemon uninstall'): cleanly disable and remove the unit/plist. Logs: route stdout/stderr to ~/.local/state/cleo/daemon/cleo-daemon.log with rotation. Depends on W1 (so daemon picks up centralized credentials). Honor CLEO_DAEMON_DISABLE=1 env to skip auto-start (for CI / containers).
acceptance: Linux: ~/.config/systemd/user/cleo-daemon.service installed + systemctl --user enable --now succeeds
macOS: ~/Library/LaunchAgents/io.cleocode.daemon.plist installed + launchctl loaded

Idempotent: re-install does not duplicate
cleo uninstall command cleanly removes the service
Logs route to ~/.local/state/cleo/daemon/cleo-daemon.log

tasks.title: Wave 2: Full MCP-as-Pi-extension bridge — real JSON-RPC client
tasks.description: Replace the v1 scaffold in PiHarness.installMcpAsExtension (which writes a stub logging 'not yet implemented' on every tool call) with a full runtime bridge. The generated Pi extension file must (a) spawn the MCP server subprocess on first tool call with the configured command/args/env, (b) speak MCP JSON-RPC over stdio (initialize → tools/list → tools/call → tool response), (c) translate each MCP tool's JSON Schema into a Pi tool definition (name, description, parameters TypeBox schema), (d) register all discovered MCP tools via pi.registerTool() dynamically at extension load time, (e) handle subprocess lifecycle (kill on session shutdown, restart on crash), (f) forward tool errors as Pi tool errors with isError: true, (g) cache the tool list so we don't re-initialize per call. Plus an SSE/HTTP transport variant for remote MCP servers. This is substantial — break into sub-tasks.
acceptance: Generated extension successfully spawns the MCP server subprocess and completes the initialize handshake

SSE and HTTP remote transports supported alongside stdio

tasks.title: backup-crypto.ts: Argon2id + AES-256-GCM encrypt/decrypt module
tasks.description: Implement packages/core/src/store/backup-crypto.ts with two exported functions: encryptBundle(tarGzBuffer, passphrase) and decryptBundle(encryptedBuffer, passphrase). Implement the binary layout from spec §7.3 (CLEOENC1 magic, format byte, 32-byte Argon2id salt, 12-byte GCM nonce, ciphertext, 16-byte auth tag). KDF parameters: Argon2id t=3, m=65536, p=4. The Argon2id library must be ADR-010 compliant (pure-JS or WASM, no native bindings). If no compliant library is found, raise a flag before proceeding. Add backup-encryption.test.ts with round-trip and failure scenarios from spec §8.1.

ADR-010 compliant: no native npm bindings in dependency chain

tasks.title: Wave 6: Dreamer Upgrade — Bayesian surprisal + consolidation specialists + hierarchical trees
tasks.description: Honcho's /mnt/projects/honcho/src/dreamer/ has three capabilities our existing brain-lifecycle.ts dream-cycle + sleep-consolidation.ts + brain-consolidator.ts lack: (a) surprisal scoring (Bayesian update — is observation surprising vs prior belief? surprising = priority consolidation), (b) topic specialists (different consolidation agents for code-patterns vs user-preferences vs decisions vs task-outcomes), (c) hierarchical memory trees (consolidated memory stored as parent-topic → child-observations tree not flat). See PLAN.md Part 4 Wave 6. Depends on Waves 1+2 (user_profile + peer_id must exist). Three children: (6.1) packages/core/src/memory/surprisal.ts — scoreSurprisal(obs priorBelief) with Bayesian update over brain_patterns with same peer_id + cosine>0.8 embedding. (6.2) sleep-consolidation.ts extension — specialist dispatch: user-preference-specialist decision-specialist code-pattern-specialist task-outcome-specialist (each a CANT agent with LLM prompt). (6.3) brain_memory_trees table + tree_id column on brain_observations — retrieval can walk tree from root → relevant subtree based on query. Honcho refs: src/dreamer/{surprisal specialists orchestrator dream_scheduler}.py + trees/

tasks.title: T-LW-W7: Daemon unification — Studio supervision, SDK control API, paths.ts compliance audit
tasks.description: Followup to T1682 (W6 daemon auto-start). Three concerns the W6 prompt didn't explicitly require: (1) the daemon must SUPERVISE Cleo Studio's web server lifecycle so one daemon process manages both sentient ticks/dreams AND the Studio HTTP server (not two separate processes); (2) public SDK API in @cleocode/core for daemon control — installDaemon(opts), uninstallDaemon(), updateDaemon(), getDaemonStatus(), startDaemon(), stopDaemon() — so SDK-only consumers (no CLI) can manage the daemon programmatically; (3) verify T1682's implementation uses packages/core/src/paths.ts for ALL filesystem paths (no hardcoded ~/.config/systemd, ~/Library, ~/.local/state) so cross-OS works on Linux/macOS/Windows-WSL. Audit T1682's implementation post-merge and remediate any hardcoded paths to use paths.ts/getPlatformPaths. Studio supervision: daemon spawns Studio as child process with restart-on-crash; daemon shutdown gracefully terminates Studio. SDK API: every CLI daemon command has an equivalent public function exported from @cleocode/core.
acceptance: Public SDK exports installDaemon/uninstallDaemon/updateDaemon/getDaemonStatus/startDaemon/stopDaemon from @cleocode/core (NOT just internal)

tasks.title: Fix injection-chain.test.ts isolation — override AGENTS_HOME in beforeEach + replace buggy inject() mock with real impl
tasks.description:
ROOT CAUSE of user's ~/.agents/AGENTS.md pollution. packages/core/src/__tests__/injection-chain.test.ts beforeEach overrides CLEO_HOME and CLEO_DIR to testDir, but does NOT override AGENTS_HOME — so getAgentsHome() returns the REAL user home. The test mock for caamp.inject() at lines 60+75 prepends '<!-- CAAMP:START -->
…
<!-- CAAMP:END -->
' + existing content WITHOUT consolidating duplicate markers. Result: every test run leaks one CAAMP block into the user's actual ~/.agents/AGENTS.md pointing at the test's temp dir. After 25+ runs, /context loads 25+ stacked blocks. Fix: (1) override AGENTS_HOME = join(testDir, '.agents') in beforeEach, restore in afterEach. (2) Replace the buggy mock — either don't mock @cleocode/caamp.inject (use the real impl) OR have the mock delegate to real injector. Depends on A3 because AGENTS_HOME factory pattern must exist first.
acceptance: beforeEach sets AGENTS_HOME to per-test sandbox
afterEach restores AGENTS_HOME
inject() mock replaced with real impl OR removed entirely
sha256 of user's ~/.agents/AGENTS.md unchanged after running pnpm test --filter injection-chain 100x

tasks.title: Wave 2: CANT .cant bridge — Pi extension loading .cant files at runtime
tasks.description: Wave 2 parent: ship the full CANT-on-Pi integration AND activate the existing WorkflowExecutor (dead code today, zero production callers). Source of truth is the Rust crates: crates/cant-core/src/dsl/{ast.rs,ast_orchestration.rs} define 8 top-level sections (Agent, Skill, Hook, Workflow, Pipeline, Import, Binding, Comment) and 16 workflow statement types inside WorkflowDef.body (Binding, Expression, Directive, Property, Comment, Session, Parallel, Conditional, Repeat, ForLoop, LoopUntil, TryCatch, ApprovalGate, Pipeline, PipeStep, Output). crates/cant-core/src/validate has 42 validation rules (S01-S13 scope, P01-P07 pipeline purity, T01-T07 types, H01-H04 hooks, W01-W11 workflow limits). crates/cant-runtime handles deterministic pipeline subprocess execution only — all LLM/discretion/approval/parallel/loops are delegated up to TypeScript per the architecture comment in crates/cant-runtime/src/lib.rs:20-37. Zero TODOs exist in any cant-* crate — Rust is production-ready. The TS layer @cleocode/cant (packages/cant/) wraps cant-napi and is shipped. The TS layer @cleocode/core cant/ namespace (packages/core/src/cant/) holds WorkflowExecutor, ApprovalManager, DefaultDiscretionEvaluator, parallel-runner, context-builder — all exported but ZERO production callers. Scope: (1) Pi extension at $CLEO_HOME/pi-extensions/cant-loader.ts that on session_start parses configured .cant files via @cleocode/cant.parseDocument, walks the AST, and registers matching Pi tools/hooks/slash commands. (2) Workflow definitions register as Pi slash commands /workflow <name> that invoke WorkflowExecutor.execute(workflowAst, ctx). (3) Hook definitions register against Pi events matching the 31 canonical CAAMP events (16 provider + 15 domain). (4) Pipeline definitions delegate to WorkflowExecutor which in turn calls cant_execute_pipeline from the napi binding (Rust runtime). BLOCKED on T287 (CANT runtime completeness epic) closing all executor gaps/stubs before integration ships — WorkflowExecutor today has 3 missing statement handlers (Expression/Property/PipeStep, closed by T289), 3 stubs (Session/Pipeline/Directive, closed by T290/T291/T292), a broken condition evaluator that always returns true for non-discretion expressions (closed by T288), and a DefaultDiscretionEvaluator hardcoded to return true (closed by T293). PiHarness gains installCantProfile/removeCantProfile/listCantProfiles methods.
acceptance: Pi extension at $CLEO_HOME/pi-extensions/cant-loader.ts loads .cant files via @cleocode/cant.parseDocument and validates via cant_validate_document (runs all 42 validation rules)
Agent definitions register as Pi tools with matching parameter schemas; permissions map to Pi tool access control
Protocol constraints inject into Pi's system prompt via before_tool_call validators; violations block the call with the protocol's error message
CANT v2 ProseBlock content renders verbatim in system prompt (preserves indentation and line breaks)
Workflow definitions register as Pi slash commands /workflow <name> that invoke WorkflowExecutor.execute() — all 16 statement types executed correctly (depends on T280 completion)
Hook definitions register as Pi event listeners matching the 31 canonical CAAMP events
Pipeline definitions invoked via WorkflowExecutor delegate to cant_execute_pipeline napi binding with correct JsPipelineResult → StepResult mapping
caamp pi cant install/remove/list/validate verbs work end-to-end honouring the three-tier scope from ADR-035 D1
Integration test: cleo-subagent.cant fixture loads in both cleo agent start (runtime path) and Pi session (extension path) and produces identical tool/hook/workflow registrations
Invalid .cant files rejected at install time with cant-core validation error messages

tasks.title: Expand RuntimeConfig and wire profile → hook dispatch in AgentPoller
tasks.description: CORE WIRE-UP: The runtime gap per T262 audit — @cleocode/runtime's createRuntime has no profile support, and AgentPoller has no hook-dispatch logic. This task adds both. Profile loading happens in cleo agent start (T296); this task just extends the runtime APIs to accept and use them. When a message arrives via poll, the dispatcher must match message.type (e.g., TaskCompleted) against HookDef.event in the loaded profile and invoke WorkflowExecutor.execute(hookBody, ctx) for each match. The 31 canonical CAAMP events are the SoT for valid event names. This is the heart of making .cant profiles actually drive agent behaviour. Parent: T287.
acceptance: RuntimeConfig in packages/runtime/src/index.ts:31 gains two new fields: profile?: ParsedCantDocument (from @cleocode/cant) and workflowExecutor?: WorkflowExecutor (from @cleocode/core/cant)
createRuntime() accepts both and stores them on the returned RuntimeHandle so downstream services can reach them

AgentPoller.handleMessage (the message dispatcher in packages/runtime/src/services/agent-poller.ts) consults the profile's hooks at dispatch time: for each incoming message, match message.type against HookDef.event and invoke WorkflowExecutor.execute() on the matched hook body
Hook events match the 31 canonical CAAMP events from crates/cant-core validation rules S06/H01 — no fuzzy matching
Multiple hooks for the same event execute in definition order (H02 forbids duplicates within one .cant file, but hooks from multiple loaded files may stack)
Errors in a hook body do not crash the poll loop — logged and reported but the agent keeps processing
Unit tests cover: no-profile (legacy behaviour preserved), profile with one hook firing on a simulated message, profile with multiple hooks, hook body with a workflow call

tasks.title: Fix RULE-5 parser: recognize CREATE TRIGGER BEGIN...END blocks as single statement (not internal-semicolon false positives)
tasks.description:
T9165 worker shipped RULE-5 in disabled-by-default state because the state-machine parser flags CREATE TRIGGER bodies as multi-statement violations. This is a parser defect, not 12 real migration defects.

EVIDENCE (verified 2026-05-07):
- packages/core/migrations/drizzle-tasks/20260503000000_t1718-handoff-trigger-syntax-fix/migration.sql has 2 actual statements (DROP TRIGGER + CREATE TRIGGER) with 1 breakpoint between them. RULE-5 incorrectly counts 3 statements because the inner 'SELECT RAISE(ABORT, ...);' is treated as a top-level statement.
- packages/core/migrations/drizzle-nexus/20260504000001_t1839-fts5-nexus-symbols/migration.sql has 7 real statements (1 CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE + 3 DROP TRIGGER + 3 CREATE TRIGGER) with 6 correct breakpoints between them. RULE-5 incorrectly counts 12 statements because the 4 trigger bodies (each containing 1-2 internal semicolons) are treated as separate statements.
- t877-pipeline-stage-invariants and t1408-archive-reason-enum likewise contain trigger bodies.

PARSER FIX REQUIRED:
The state-machine in scripts/lint-migrations.mjs countSqlStatements() must recognize:
  CREATE TRIGGER ... BEGIN <body-with-semicolons> END;

When the parser sees 'BEGIN' (case-insensitive, top-level), it enters TRIGGER_BODY state. Internal semicolons increment statement count by ZERO until the matching 'END;' (case-insensitive) is seen. Then the parser exits TRIGGER_BODY state.

NESTING: SQLite triggers do not nest CREATE TRIGGER. Other BEGIN keywords (e.g. BEGIN TRANSACTION) are not allowed inside trigger bodies. Single-level state suffices.

ALSO HANDLE: 'CREATE TEMP TRIGGER', 'CREATE TEMPORARY TRIGGER', 'CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS' variants.

Once parser is fixed and zero violations exist after T9166 + this task land, flip RULE-5 to default-on (remove --enable-rule-5 gate; replace with --disable-rule-5 emergency bypass that emits deprecation warning).

NO BANDAIDS: do not add an allowlist of 'expected violations'. Do not weaken the rule. Fix the parser correctly so it accurately reflects the SQL semantics.
acceptance: scripts/lint-migrations.mjs RULE-5 state-machine parser recognizes 'BEGIN ... END;' blocks (case-insensitive) and treats their internal semicolons as non-statement-separators
RULE-5 with --enable-rule-5 produces ZERO violations across drizzle-tasks, drizzle-brain (post-T9166), drizzle-nexus, drizzle-conduit, drizzle-signaldock, drizzle-telemetry
RULE-5 default-on flipped (remove --enable-rule-5 gating; flag becomes --disable-rule-5 for emergency bypass with deprecation warning)

Self-tested on the 5 trigger-bearing migrations: t877-pipeline-stage-invariants t1408-archive-reason-enum t1609-handoff-append-only t1718-handoff-trigger-syntax-fix t1839-fts5-nexus-symbols — all pass after parser fix
Existing RULE-1 through RULE-4 still pass
GitHub Actions migration-lint job continues to use --fail-on=error and now fails on real violations with no false positives
Post-T9166 brain migrations also produce zero RULE-5 violations

tasks.title: Wave 3: Orchestrator mode — PiHarness.spawnSubagent as primary subagent entrypoint
tasks.description: The v1 PiHarness.spawnSubagent exists but is minimal — it spawns 'pi --mode json -p --no-session "<prompt>"' and collects stdout. Upgrade it to a full orchestrator entrypoint: (1) streaming stdout via line-delimited JSON (vitest-style event emissions) so callers can observe progress in real time, (2) structured error propagation — parse JSON error envelopes from Pi and surface them as typed SubagentErrors, (3) session attribution — pass a parent session id to the child via env var so Pi logs the subagent session as a child of the parent, (4) multi-agent fan-out — Promise.all over an array of SubagentTasks with proper concurrency limits, (5) abort signal propagation — parent SIGTERM → child SIGTERM, (6) working directory and env isolation per subagent.
acceptance: streaming stdout emits events as Pi produces them, not buffered until exit
SubagentError class distinguishes network errors, Pi errors, tool errors, and abort
Parent→child session id propagation verified via Pi's session metadata
Multi-agent fan-out runs up to N (default 4) subagents concurrently with proper cleanup on failure
abort() on the parent propagates SIGTERM to all active children within 5s
Integration test spawns 3 subagents (claude-code, cursor, codex targets via --agent flag) and asserts all three receive their prompt and return output
