Fix it: parse via @cleocode/cant, instantiate WorkflowExecutor (from @cleocode/core cant/) with the default DiscretionEvaluator (from T293) and ApprovalManager, and pass both to createRuntime() (extended in T295). ALSO ship the comprehensive unit test suite for WorkflowExecutor that the T262 audit flagged as missing (ZERO workflow execution tests exist today — only skipped Layer 1 parse tests). Parent: T287.
acceptance: packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/agent.ts:258-262 no longer drops the profile string — parses it via @cleocode/cant.parseDocument
cleo agent start instantiates WorkflowExecutor from @cleocode/core/cant with a provider-backed DiscretionEvaluator and the default ApprovalManager
createRuntime call at agent.ts:310 gains profile and workflowExecutor fields from the parsed profile and instantiated executor
Profile validation errors at startup produce a clear error message with the failing rule ID and line/col — agent does NOT start with an invalid profile

Comprehensive WorkflowExecutor unit test suite at packages/core/src/cant/__tests__/workflow-executor.test.ts covering: (a) every statement type executes correctly in isolation, (b) all 3 parallel join strategies (all, race, settle) with the parallel-runner, (c) approval token state machine (generate, validate, approve, reject, expire, TOCTOU protection), (d) scope chain variable resolution (parent, child, override), (e) template interpolation (single-pass, missing variable, nested forbidden), (f) discretion evaluation via MockDiscretionEvaluator, (g) try/catch/finally with exception propagation, (h) loop constructs (repeat, for, loop-until with max-iteration guard)
Integration test loads cleo-subagent.cant via cleo agent start and verifies the agent actually reaches its poll loop with the profile wired
All 8 existing .cant fixture files load cleanly in the integration test
Test file at packages/core/src/cant/__tests__/cant-agent-parse.test.ts is un-skipped now that parseDocument is wired

tasks.title: Wave 3: Integration test suite for database topology
tasks.description: Wave 3 of T299 — final verification task before release. End-to-end integration tests covering all 9 topology scenarios. Each scenario exercises a specific contract from ADR-036. Depends on all Wave 1-2 tasks being complete so the test can assert the new contracts.
acceptance: New file packages/core/src/store/__tests__/database-topology-integration.test.ts
Scenario 1: fresh init creates tasks.db + brain.db at project tier and nexus.db at global tier (XDG); verify paths match getPlatformPaths() output
Scenario 2: walk-up from a nested subdir finds the correct project root and uses its .cleo/; walk-up from orphan dir refuses with E_NO_PROJECT
Scenario 3: walk-up in a nested dir with a parent .cleo/ and an ancestor .cleo/ above (two projects in an ancestor chain) STOPS at first hit (nearest) and does not drift
Scenario 4: cleo backup add snapshots all project files; cleo backup add --global snapshots global nexus.db; both via VACUUM INTO
Scenario 5: corrupt tasks.db then cleo restore backup → verify recovery preserves all rows (integrity_check ok)
Scenario 6: cleanup-legacy.ts idempotent run on filesystem with legacy files present → all deleted; re-run → no-op
Scenario 7: nested .cleo/ is NEVER created by a post-v2026.4.11 cleo command; verify via running cleo from a nested subdir and checking file system
Scenario 8: stray project .cleo/nexus.db → detected and removed by cleanup-legacy, guard assertion prevents regeneration
Scenario 9: CLEO_ROOT env var override works and bypasses walk-up
All 9 scenarios pass; test file runs in under 30 seconds; uses tmp dirs + mocked getCleoHome() for isolation
No source changes in this task (test file only)

tasks.title: api-key-kdf.ts: deriveApiKey and deriveLegacyProjectKey
tasks.description: Create packages/core/src/store/api-key-kdf.ts with the two KDF functions from spec §3.4. deriveApiKey(opts: { machineKey, globalSalt, agentId }) uses HMAC-SHA256(key=machineKey||globalSalt, message=agentId) returning a 32-byte Buffer. deriveLegacyProjectKey(opts: { machineKey, projectPath }) mirrors the existing credentials.ts::deriveProjectKey logic (HMAC-SHA256(machineKey, projectPath)) for use during migration decryption. Both functions are pure (no I/O). Also add unit tests in store/__tests__/api-key-kdf.test.ts covering TC-040 through TC-046.
acceptance: deriveApiKey returns a 32-byte Buffer and is deterministic for same inputs (TC-040, TC-041)
deriveApiKey output differs when agentId, globalSalt, or machineKey differ (TC-042, TC-043, TC-044)
deriveLegacyProjectKey matches credentials.ts::deriveProjectKey output for same inputs (TC-045)
deriveApiKey output differs from deriveLegacyProjectKey for same agent — confirms KDF migration (TC-046)
Both functions are pure with no I/O side effects

tasks.title: backup-unpack.ts: bundle extraction and all 6 integrity verification layers
tasks.description: Implement packages/core/src/store/backup-unpack.ts with exported function unpackBundle(bundlePath: string, options: UnpackOptions): Promise<UnpackResult>. Implement all 6 integrity layers from spec §4.2 in strict sequence: Layer 1 AES-256-GCM auth tag (encrypted bundles only, delegates to backup-crypto.ts), Layer 2 manifest hash verification (manifestHash='' substitution), Layer 3 manifest schema validation against embedded schemas/manifest-v1.json, Layer 4 per-file SHA-256 checksums, Layer 5 SQLite PRAGMA integrity_check (reuse validateSqliteDatabase from packages/core/src/store/atomic.ts), Layer 6 schema version comparison with warnings. Exit on any failure before touching live files. Add backup-unpack.test.ts covering all spec §8.1 scenarios. Files: backup-unpack.ts + backup-unpack.test.ts.
acceptance: All 6 integrity layers execute in strict order; failure at any layer aborts with correct exit code (70-75)
Tampered checksums.sha256 entry produces E_CHECKSUM_MISMATCH (72) reporting the failing file name
Missing manifest.json produces E_MANIFEST_MISSING (74); missing schemas/manifest-v1.json produces E_SCHEMAS_MISSING (75)
Schema version mismatch emits WARNING only; import is not blocked (Q5=C)
No live files are touched on any integrity failure

tasks.title: HOTFIX v2026.4.111: systemic — move starter-bundle out of cleo-os + resolver fallback + CI green
tasks.description:
Owner-directive 2026-04-22: v2026.4.110 shipped 3 regressions I claimed green. Fix SYSTEMICALLY not symptomatically. Per D035 (just filed): packages/cleo-os/starter-bundle/ is architectural violation — universal agents MUST live in packages/agents/ and seed-install MUST read from there. 

CI failure evidence: 5 tests in orchestrate-engine-composer.test.ts + orchestrate-engine.test.ts. AssertionError: expected E_ATOMICITY_NO_SCOPE received E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND. Root cause: I1 removed cleo-prime/cleo-dev/cleo-historian/cleo-db-lead/cleo-rust-lead/cleoos-opus-orchestrator from packages/agents/seed-agents/ (correct per owner). Classifier picks them. Resolver walks 4 tiers. CI clean env has zero registry rows + zero fs matches. E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND surfaces BEFORE atomicity gate can reject. Tests expect atomicity error.

Deliverables (systemic not symptomatic):

A. RELOCATE packages/cleo-os/starter-bundle/ → packages/agents/seed-agents/. The 4 files (cleo-orchestrator.cant code-worker.cant dev-lead.cant docs-worker.cant) either merge INTO the *-generic.cant templates OR become additional templates with {{variable}} placeholders. team.cant → packages/agents/team-generic.cant. starter/ subdirectory pattern → packages/agents/starter/ at root.

B. ROUTE seed-install: packages/core/src/agents/seed-install.ts reads from packages/agents/ via new SDK helper resolveStarterBundle() in core. NEVER from packages/cleo-os/. Applies variable-substitution.ts during copy to .cleo/cant/agents/. packages/cleo-os/ retains ONLY harness-adapter code (Claude Code AGENT.md wrapper belongs at packages/agents/harness-adapters/claude-code/).

C. RESOLVER ULTIMATE FALLBACK: packages/core/src/store/agent-resolver.ts — when all 4 tiers fail for a classified agent name (project + global + packaged + synthetic), fall back to synthesizing ResolvedAgent from packages/agents/cleo-subagent.cant (universal protocol base). Document as 5th tier 'universal-base' in DEPRECATED_ALIASES-adjacent map. Emit warning but NEVER E_AGENT_NOT_FOUND if cleo-subagent is reachable.

D. FIX 5 FAILING TESTS: via fallback from (C) they will now pass — classifier picks cleo-prime, resolver falls through to universal base, atomicity gate fires expected E_ATOMICITY_NO_SCOPE. Verify locally + verify CI green after push.

E. REGISTRY CLEANUP: after relocation the old packages/cleo-os paths in agents registry rows become stale. Add migration step that re-points project-tier rows to packages/agents/ paths. cleo agent doctor must return 0 warnings post-migration.

F. DOCS: update ADR-055 with D035 addendum, CHANGELOG entry for v2026.4.111, docs/meta-agents.md cross-ref to D035 if needed.

G. RELEASE: bump to 2026.4.111, push, verify CI green, install globally, verify cleo --version.

Work in worktree per D029. All paths via paths.ts + platform-paths.ts + env-paths per D026. Package-Boundary Check enforced.
acceptance: packages/cleo-os/starter-bundle/ moved to packages/agents/ (deleted from cleo-os) — or kept as read-only re-export shim with deprecation warning
packages/core/src/agents/seed-install.ts reads from packages/agents/ ONLY (zero imports from packages/cleo-os/)
packages/core/src/agents/resolveStarterBundle.ts SDK helper ships + is used by seed-install
packages/core/src/store/agent-resolver.ts has 5th-tier universal-base fallback pointing at packages/agents/cleo-subagent.cant
All 5 failing tests green locally AND in CI after push (orchestrate-engine-composer.test.ts + orchestrate-engine.test.ts)

Fresh project test: mkdir /tmp/fresh && cd /tmp/fresh && git init && cleo init → .cleo/cant/agents/ has 4 substituted starter agents with {{variable}} values resolved from project-context.json (not literal {{placeholder}})
cleo agent doctor returns 0 warnings post-migration
D035 filed + ADR-055 updated with addendum
CHANGELOG v2026.4.111 entry explicit about the architectural move + resolver fallback
biome ci clean + pnpm build full green + pnpm test zero new failures
v2026.4.111 tagged + pushed + globally installed + cleo --version returns 2026.4.111

tasks.title: T-HR-S5: Sentient hygiene background loop (dreams + sleep cycles)
tasks.description: Wire BRAIN dream/sleep cycles to perform full hygiene reviews automatically. Each dream cycle (sentient tick): (1) scan for orphan tasks (parentId pointing to done/cancelled/missing) -> emit observations; (2) scan for top-level type=task tasks -> emit observations recommending re-parent or epic-promote; (3) scan content quality (missing AC, missing files, vague AC) -> emit observations; (4) scan for premature-close cases (slip past S1 invariant somehow) -> emit critical observations; (5) compose a daily hygiene digest accessible via cleo memory digest --hygiene. This is the LLM-agent-under-the-hood the user wants — automatic system organization without manual audits.

tasks.title: Wave 4 (v3): CAAMP Pi-exclusive dispatch — route through Pi when installed
tasks.description:
**PARTIAL — 70% shipped in v2026.4.5, ~30% gap remaining.**

v2026.4.5 registered Pi with priority: "primary" in packages/caamp/providers/registry.json:1859 and wired resolveDefaultTargetProviders() in packages/caamp/src/core/harness/index.ts:143. The function is used across 8 command handlers (skills/install, skills/list, skills/remove, instructions/inject, instructions/check, instructions/update, advanced/common — see grep results). Current behaviour: when no --agent flag is supplied, default to Pi if installed; otherwise fall through to other providers.

GAP for v3 exclusivity: there is no exclusivityMode config setting and no way to FORCE Pi exclusivity or DISABLE it. Explicit --agent <other> still works without warning.

Rescoped scope: add a caamp.exclusivityMode setting with three values (auto/force-pi/legacy). 'auto' (default) = current behaviour (default-to-Pi if installed) + deprecation warning when user explicitly targets a non-Pi provider. 'force-pi' = error at startup if Pi is not installed. 'legacy' = current behaviour without the warning. Skill/instruction install paths stay unchanged (per ADR-035 D7) — this only affects runtime invocation. Small scope, ~200 LOC, no new packages.
acceptance: caamp.exclusivityMode setting added to caamp config schema with enum auto
force-pi
legacy, default auto
resolveDefaultTargetProviders honours the mode
force-pi mode exits with status 4 + E_NOT_FOUND_RESOURCE if Pi absent at startup
auto mode emits a single deprecation warning per process when --agent <non-pi> is supplied
legacy mode matches v2026.4.5 behaviour exactly (no warning, no exclusivity)
Skill/instruction install paths are NOT affected — dispatching to multiple providers for install still works
Unit tests cover all 3 modes × Pi-installed/absent matrix (6 scenarios)
Integration test: caamp skills list --agent claude-code in auto mode emits warning but still succeeds
notes: 2026-04-07 13:40:27 UTC: The v3 irreversible collapse. Requires Wave 3 orchestrator mode to route other-provider work through Pi, otherwise generic providers are unreachable.

2026-04-07 19:16:19 UTC: 2026-04-07 19:15 UTC: Audit against current repo shows 70% already shipped. Rescoped to add the exclusivityMode setting + warning layer. No exclusivity enforcement for non-runtime install paths — matches ADR-035 D7 decision.

tasks.description: Implement packages/core/src/store/regenerators.ts exposing three read-only dry-run generator functions: regenerateConfigJson(projectRoot), regenerateProjectInfoJson(projectRoot), regenerateProjectContextJson(projectRoot). tasks.title: Wave 9: Conduit Agent-to-Agent Communication Integration
tasks.description: OWNER-FLAGGED 2026-04-21: None of the spawned agents use agent-to-agent communication. CONDUIT exists in packages/cleo-os + packages/core but the spawn model is one-shot manifest return. For the Honcho multi-peer architecture (Waves 1-8) to work as designed agents MUST coordinate peer-to-peer. Without A2A: (a) Agent Lead on Wave 2 can't notify Wave 3 Lead when peer_id column lands → serial-only execution, (b) dialectic-evaluator peer-insights can't ping the peer-card specialist to trigger representation update, (c) dreamer specialists can't share consolidation findings → duplicated work, (d) reconciler dead-letter can't notify originating agent to replay with context. Four children. (9.1) Audit current Conduit implementation — what exists under packages/core/src/store/conduit-sqlite.ts vs packages/cleo-os/, what operations are exposed via dispatch, what tests exist. (9.2) Design Peer→Peer message protocol — extend CONDUIT envelope with {fromPeerId toPeerId threadId kind: request|notify|subscribe payload}. (9.3) Wire spawn prompt to include CONDUIT subscribe instructions — spawn tier 2 prompts embed 'subscribe to topic <epicId>.<waveId>' + 'publish findings to topic <epicId>.coordination'. (9.4) Orchestrator coordination room — meta-topic where Leads publish wave-complete notifications that other Leads listen for to unblock their wave. References D023 SDK-first: Conduit operations must be core SDK primitives first CLI after.
acceptance: CONDUIT-AUDIT.md written mapping all existing conduit paths/ops/tests

tasks.title: Wave 5 (v3): CLEO dispatch layer routes through Pi as sole orchestrator
tasks.description:
**NOT STARTED. Architectural question more than implementation work.**

Audit 2026-04-07: grep for PiHarness, getPrimaryHarness, dispatchInstallSkillAcrossProviders in packages/cleo/src/dispatch returned ZERO matches. CLEO's dispatch layer (packages/cleo/src/dispatch/domains/*.ts) does NOT route anything through Pi. CLI handlers call domain functions directly. Even the Conductor Loop (orchestrator.ts Pi extension) shells out to 'cleo agent work --execute' rather than wrapping CLEO operations through Pi.

The original T279 scope was 'CLEO dispatch layer routes through Pi as sole orchestrator — other providers are pure spawn targets.' That's a significant architectural change with low payoff: the current architecture (CLEO CLI handles task/session/memory ops directly via dispatch; Pi handles interactive orchestration via Conductor Loop + cant-bridge) is arguably CLEANER than forcing everything through Pi. Pi is the CANT workflow runtime and the interactive harness; it is NOT a replacement for CLEO's CLI dispatch of tasks/sessions/memory.

Recommended action: CLOSE this task with status 'superseded — architecture clarified 2026-04-07' after owner confirms. Alternative: rescope to 'CLEO domain handlers expose a 'pi-native' flag per operation that routes specific interactive operations through Pi when appropriate' — but that's speculative and adds complexity.

AWAITING OWNER DECISION on whether to close or rescope.
acceptance: When Pi is installed: cleo skills install/cleo agent run/cleo task start etc. all route through PiHarness.spawnSubagent or direct harness methods
When Pi is absent: CLEO falls back to the pre-v3 direct dispatch (no regression for users without Pi)
packages/cleo/src/dispatch/ has no provider-specific branching for skills/MCP/instructions — all goes through CAAMP or Pi
Recursive orchestration: Pi can spawn CLEO as a subagent which can spawn further subagents
End-to-end test: cleo skills install <source> from a Pi-installed env routes through Pi and installs into Pi's skills dir only
Release workflow ships v3 across all 10 @cleocode/* packages
notes: 2026-04-07 13:40:51 UTC: The final collapse. After this, the 44 generic providers exist in CAAMP's registry solely as Pi spawn targets — they are never written to by CLEO or CAAMP directly.

2026-04-07 19:16:20 UTC: 2026-04-07 19:15 UTC: Audit shows zero Pi routing in cleo dispatch. Original v3 scope is questionable architecturally. Recommended: close as superseded. Pending owner confirmation.

tasks.title: restore-json-merge.ts: A/B classification engine with 4-way taxonomy
tasks.description: Implement packages/core/src/store/restore-json-merge.ts with the exported function regenerateAndCompare(input: JsonRestoreInput): JsonRestoreReport. Implement the full 5-category classification taxonomy from spec §6.3: identical, machine-local (A wins), user-intent (B wins), project-identity (B wins), auto-detect (A wins), unknown (manual-review). Apply resolution precedence order: identical > machine-local > user-intent > project-identity > auto-detect > unknown. Delegates to regenerators.ts for local generation (A). Calls to JsonRestoreInput.projectRoot for dry-run generation. Implement deep-path traversal with dot-notation field paths. Use TypeScript interfaces from packages/contracts/src/backup-manifest.ts. Add restore-json-merge.test.ts covering all 5 categories and spec §8.3 scenarios. Files: restore-json-merge.ts + restore-json-merge.test.ts.
acceptance: All 5 classification categories produce correct resolution: machine-local keeps A, user-intent keeps B, project-identity keeps B, auto-detect keeps A, unknown flags manual-review
Identical fields (JSON.stringify A === JSON.stringify B) produce 'identical' category and do not appear in the conflict report
Nested dot-notation paths (e.g. 'brain.embeddingProvider') are traversed and classified correctly
regenerateAndCompare does NOT write anything to disk; returns a JsonRestoreReport

tasks.title: T-MSR-META-01: Bug — subagents can unilaterally advance parent epic lifecycle through all stages to bypass gate checks
tasks.description: During T1150 RCASD orchestration on 2026-04-21 a subagent (likely T1159 W0-3 runtime-guard worker encountering E_LIFECYCLE_GATE_FAILED when trying to call cleo complete on its own task) advanced the T1150 epic through EVERY lifecycle stage from research to release within 75 seconds (17:59:17 → 18:00:32). This completely bypassed the RCASD gate machinery that is supposed to force Research→Consensus→ArchitectureDecision→Specification→Decomposition→Implementation before children can complete. Orchestrator-side state desynced from epic stage; downstream RECOMMENDATION.md ceremony still required despite lifecycle claiming release:completed. Risk surface: any adversarial or mis-configured subagent can fast-forward epic lifecycle and bypass owner checkpoints. Per ADR-051 evidence must be programmatic — stage advancement should be evidence-gated too. Severity P1 because RCASD is the governance backbone for owner HITL oversight. Found during T1150 Hybrid Path A+ orchestration.
acceptance: Reproduce: cleo lifecycle history T1150 shows research→consensus→architecture_decision→specification→decomposition→implementation→validation→testing→release all completed within 75s window (17:59:17 to 18:00:32 on 2026-04-21) by a subagent attempting to pass its E_LIFECYCLE_GATE_FAILED gate
Root cause investigation: identify which cleo command path lets a subagent advance lifecycle stages on a parent epic when it only has permission for its own task scope
Surface the vector: document whether this is cleo lifecycle complete (meant to be owner+orchestrator only) vs cleo verify side-effect vs session hook

Design fix: lifecycle stage advancement for an epic must require either (a) explicit ownership of the epic task b) orchestrator role flag or (c) HITL approval — NEVER a transitive side-effect of a child task verify/complete
Add test: spawn subagent scoped to child task attempts cleo lifecycle complete on parent epic — must be rejected with E_SCOPE_DENIED
Document in ADR-054 or a separate governance ADR why this is load-bearing (RCASD integrity requires stages to gate work not be bypassed by workers)
Evidence atoms required for the fix commit

tasks.title: P2 residual: evaluate swap of pure-TS leiden.ts → @aflsolutions/graphology-communities-leiden
tasks.description:
Currently packages/nexus/src/pipeline/leiden.ts is a 405-line pure-TS Leiden implementation, written 2026-04-20 because graphology-leiden was 404 on npm. On 2026-04-26, @aflsolutions/graphology-communities-leiden v1.1.1 was published (Leiden community detection algorithm for graphology, extracted from the graphology monorepo by blade47). The maintained package landed 6 days after cleo's pure-TS impl. This task evaluates whether to swap.

Tradeoffs to assess:
- Stay with pure-TS: zero-deps (matches operator preference), 405 lines we fully own, already shipped + tested
- Swap to @aflsolutions: lower maintenance burden, battle-tested upstream extraction, likely better perf (graphology-optimized), one more scoped dep

Decision criteria:
- IF Decision 3 (T1042 functional validation campaign) shows cleo's Leiden lags gitnexus' Leiden meaningfully (>1.5x partition quality regression on /mnt/projects/openclaw or comparable benchmark), the swap becomes a cheap fix — do it
- IF parity is close, the 405 lines we own are an asset (zero-deps wins), keep pure-TS
- Either way, document the decision in a follow-up commit message + observation

Trigger: Run only after the Decision 3 functional validation task completes (it produces the partition-quality measurement this task needs).
acceptance: Functional validation data from Decision 3 task is available before this task starts
Run @aflsolutions/graphology-communities-leiden against the same fixture (/mnt/projects/openclaw or equivalent) used by Decision 3
Compare 5 axes: (1) wall-clock perf, (2) modularity score, (3) community count, (4) memory footprint, (5) bundle size impact
Decision documented in either a commit message rationale OR a council-style observation IF outcome is non-obvious
If swap chosen: pure-TS leiden.ts deleted, @aflsolutions/... added to packages/nexus dependencies, all community-process tests still green
If keep chosen: a comment block at top of leiden.ts updated to note the maintained alternative exists and was evaluated as not worth the dep cost

tasks.title: ADR: CAAMP↔adapters boundary — CAAMP owns instruction-file injection + provider registry; adapters own provider-specific concerns
tasks.description: Lock the architectural boundary now that B1+B2 prove the shape. Write ADR-0XX (next sequential ADR number) documenting: (1) CAAMP package owns the provider registry (single source of truth for instructFile, instructionReferences, mcp config keys, capabilities) AND the marker-based injection engine (inject, ensureProviderInstructionFile, checkInjection); (2) Adapters package owns provider-specific runtime concerns (spawn behavior, statusline sync, hook templates, plugin registration, context monitoring) — adapters CONSUME CAAMP for any provider-metadata or instruction-file work, never reimplement; (3) CLEO core consumes CAAMP API and supplies dynamic reference lists; (4) @cleocode/paths owns all platform/XDG path resolution — both CAAMP and adapters and CLEO consume it. ADR makes future drift detectable. Filed after B2 because real refactor exposes any boundary edge cases the doc must address.

tasks.title: Fix Node 24 WriteStream validation in sentient daemon spawn
tasks.description: Node v24 changed child_process.spawn to validate stdio WriteStream file descriptors synchronously. The sentient daemon's log streams were created with createWriteStream but not awaited for the 'open' event before being passed to spawn(). This caused ERR_INVALID_FD_TYPE on Node 24. Fix: import { once } from 'node:events' and await Promise.all([once(outStream, 'open'), once(errStream, 'open')]) before spawn().
acceptance: Node 24 child_process.spawn no longer throws ERR_INVALID_FD_TYPE on stdio WriteStreams
sentient daemon starts successfully without stdio validation errors
Change is committed atomically with clear provenance in commit message

tasks.title: PSYCHE LLM Layer Port — port Honcho src/llm (5680 LOC) as CLEO multi-model abstraction
tasks.description: New wave (propose Wave 10) per D033 port-strategy. Honcho's src/llm/ subsystem (5680 LOC across 13 files) is the biggest single port opportunity — gives CLEO baked-in multi-model abstraction + caching + tool-loop + structured-output + retry in one port. Replaces our thin packages/core/src/memory/llm-backend-resolver.ts. DIRECT-PORT files: executor.py → packages/core/src/llm/executor.ts; tool_loop.py → packages/core/src/llm/tool-loop.ts; request_builder.py → packages/core/src/llm/request-builder.ts. ADAPT files: caching.py (Gemini-specific handle store) → packages/core/src/llm/cache.ts (provider-agnostic + Redis/memcached hook); structured_output.py (Pydantic→Zod; 3-tier fallback valid/repair/policy); registry.py (DI-friendly pattern); api.py (Tenacity→custom retry; ContextVar→AsyncLocalStorage); backends/ (anthropic/openai/gemini implementations). Satisfies Pillar 4 (model-agnosticism) in one port. Depends on: T1142 (Honcho source audit) + T1144.0.2 (agents peer_id SSoT) already shipped. Blocks: T1087 Wave 3 dialectic evaluator (ported dialectic/core.py adapts best when llm/ exists); T1145 Wave 5 deriver queue (queue_manager.py depends on executor pattern).
acceptance: packages/core/src/llm/ ships with executor tool-loop request-builder cache structured-output registry api modules
Zod schemas replace Pydantic throughout ported code
Provider registry supports claude-4 gpt-5 gemini at minimum with backend-adapter pattern
tool-loop.ts implements iteration retry + empty-response retry + tool-result accumulation (port of execute_tool_loop)

tasks.title: Bump openai SDK 4 → 6 to eliminate node-fetch/punycode deprecation chain
tasks.description:
Current state: packages/core/package.json pins openai@^4.0.0 (resolves to 4.104.0). The transitive chain pulls in node-fetch@2.7.0 → whatwg-url@5.0.0 → tr46@0.0.3 → require('punycode'), which emits Node 21+ DEP0040 deprecation warnings on every cleo CLI invocation.

openai@6.35.0 (latest) has zero runtime dependencies — they migrated to native fetch in v5 and dropped node-fetch entirely. Bumping eliminates the entire chain.

Surface to migrate (small, contained):
- packages/core/src/llm/registry.ts — 4 instantiation sites: 'new OpenAI({apiKey})', 'new OpenAI({apiKey, baseURL})' for moonshot
- packages/core/src/llm/backends/openai.ts — 3 call sites: 'client.chat.completions.create(...)' (one streaming, two non-streaming)
- packages/core/src/llm/backends/moonshot.ts — extends OpenAIBackend, no direct API calls
- packages/core/src/llm/types.ts — type-only import (OpenAI type)
- packages/core/src/llm/__tests__/llm-layer.test.ts — imports usesMaxCompletionTokens helper

Migration notes (4 → 6 known breaking changes):
- v5: dropped Completions endpoint (we don't use it), error class hierarchy changed, response shape on some streaming events changed
- v6: tightened types, reduced methods on the client root namespace (we don't use those)
- Constructor unchanged: new OpenAI({apiKey, baseURL}) still works
- chat.completions.create signature stable for the common params (model, messages, stream, temperature, max_tokens, tools)

Verification: 'pnpm why punycode' should NOT show whatwg-url@5/tr46@0.0.3 in the dependency tree after the bump. The DEP0040 warning should be gone from 'cleo --version' output.
acceptance: openai dependency in packages/core/package.json bumped from ^4.0.0 to ^6.0.0
All 4 source files (registry.ts, backends/openai.ts, backends/moonshot.ts, types.ts) compile under the new SDK without 'as any' casts
pnpm why punycode shows the openai chain is GONE (only @types/node may still match, that is fine)
cleo --version invocation produces NO DEP0040 deprecation warning on stderr
All llm/__tests__/* tests still pass: pnpm --filter @cleocode/core test llm -- --run
biome+typecheck+build all green
If any v5/v6 breaking change requires a code-shape change (e.g. response handling), the change is noted in the commit message

tasks.title: Add session-end Observer hook unconditional run
tasks.description: GAP-6: Observer only fires on task completion when count >= threshold (default 10). Sessions with < 10 observations never get compression. tasks.title: Fix Node 24 WriteStream validation in GC daemon spawn
tasks.description: The GC daemon has the same Node 24 WriteStream bug as the sentient daemon. It creates outStream/errStream with createWriteStream and passes them directly to spawn() without awaiting the 'open' event. Apply the identical fix: import { once } from 'node:events' and await Promise.all([once(outStream, 'open'), once(errStream, 'open')]) before spawn().
acceptance: GC daemon starts successfully without stdio validation errors on Node 24
Same once('open') pattern applied consistently with sentient daemon
Change is committed atomically with clear provenance in commit message

tasks.title: CAAMP writer-bypass: temp-path injection still accumulates 37+ blocks despite T1939 dedup-by-path fix
tasks.description: Follow-up to T1939. T1939 made the CAAMP writer dedup blocks BY PATH (same @path = upsert, not append). But CLEO sessions keep generating new temp-path injection chains under ~/.temp/cleo-injection-chain-XXXXXX/, and each session's NEW unique temp path triggers a new block append per T1939's contract. Result: ~/.agents/AGENTS.md grew to 37 unique blocks (was deduped from 41 just hours ago). The fix is upstream of dedupe — either don't inject temp paths at all, or GC stale temp-path blocks on session start. Observed during T1932 dispatch on 2026-05-06.
acceptance: Root-cause analysis: identify the CAAMP writer code path that bypasses T1939's idempotent writer when injecting via ~/.temp/cleo-injection-chain-XXXXXX paths

Fix: writer must NOT create new injection blocks for temp paths that will become stale after process exit (either: skip temp-path injection entirely, OR garbage-collect temp-path blocks on next session start)
Test: simulate 5 sequential CLEO sessions with different temp dirs, verify final ~/.agents/AGENTS.md does NOT accumulate 5 stale blocks
Test: existing dedupe-by-path test from T1939 still passes (no regression)
Live cleanup: run cleo caamp dedupe + manual filter on ~/.agents/AGENTS.md to drop stale temp-path blocks; document the canonical block set
biome check + pnpm run test green

fs writes to AGENTS.md cascade only - no new network surface
live agents AGENTS.md confirmed clean - 1 canonical block kept, T1939 dedup contract preserved

tasks.title: migrate-signaldock-to-conduit.ts: 15-step migration executor
tasks.description: Create packages/core/src/store/migrate-signaldock-to-conduit.ts implementing the full 15-step migration sequence from spec §4. Exports: needsMigration(projectRoot) detection heuristic; runMigration(projectRoot) executing all 15 steps with correct error handling and rollback per spec §4.4. Steps include: READ-ONLY open legacy DB, PRAGMA integrity_check, ensureGlobalSignaldockDb, getGlobalSalt, read machine-key, ensureConduitDb, TRANSACTION copy project-tier tables + rebuild FTS + derive project_agent_refs rows, COMMIT, integrity_check conduit, TRANSACTION copy global-identity rows with KDF re-keying using deriveLegacyProjectKey + deriveApiKey, COMMIT global, integrity_check global, atomic rename to .pre-t310.bak, INFO/WARN logs. All failure cases per spec §4.4 rollback matrix. Covers spec §4.2-§4.5. Add unit tests TC-060 through TC-073. Files: migrate-signaldock-to-conduit.ts (new), store/__tests__/migrate-signaldock-to-conduit.test.ts.
acceptance: needsMigration returns false when conduit.db exists or signaldock.db absent; true when both conditions met (TC-060, TC-061, TC-062)
Migration with 0 agents creates conduit.db + global signaldock.db + .pre-t310.bak (TC-063)
Migration with 2 agents migrates both to global; 2 project_agent_refs rows created; api_key_encrypted re-keyed (TC-064, TC-065)
Second project migrating same agentId uses INSERT OR IGNORE without overwriting global row (TC-066)
Legacy integrity_check failure aborts migration with no .pre-t310.bak created and conduit.db absent (TC-068)

tasks.title: CLI: cleo backup import command with A/B flow and atomic DB restore
tasks.description: Implement packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/backup-import.ts as the cleo backup import <bundle> subcommand. Wire --force flag to bypass E_DATA_EXISTS pre-check. Flow: (1) pre-check live data per spec §5.2 step 1, abort E_DATA_EXISTS (78) without --force; (2) detect encryption from extension, prompt passphrase if .enc; (3) unpackBundle via backup-unpack.ts; (4) atomic tmp-then-rename DB writes, clear WAL sidecars; (5) run regenerateAndCompare for each JSON file; (6) writeConflictReport; (7) log result. Register on backup.ts. Add backup-import.test.ts CLI integration test. Files: backup-import.ts (new) + backup.ts (register only).
acceptance: Import into empty target: all DBs and JSON files restored; .cleo/restore-conflicts.md written; exit 0
Import into live target without --force: E_DATA_EXISTS (78); lists conflicting files; nothing overwritten
Import with --force: overwrites existing data; A/B comparison still runs for JSON files
Encrypted bundle import with correct passphrase: successful decrypt and restore
Encrypted bundle import with wrong passphrase: E_BUNDLE_DECRYPT (70); nothing written to disk

tasks.title: T-MSR-W2A-03: Baseline-reset snapshot chains for tasks/brain/nexus — probe-and-skip baseline migration per DB
tasks.description: Hybrid Path A+ Wave 2A-iii (critical path). **ADDITIONAL CONSTRAINT** per T1163 findings: existing snapshot.json files for tasks/brain/telemetry are malformed (version 7 missing 'renames' key per beta.22 format). Baseline reset must regenerate snapshots in CORRECT beta.22 format. Run drizzle-kit generate in a mode that produces fresh correctly-formatted snapshots — the output replaces the malformed old snapshots. For each DB: (1) generate baseline via W2A-02 script, (2) capture the FRESH snapshot.json (KEEP IT — replaces malformed old one), (3) replace migration.sql contents with '-- BASELINE MARKER — schema state captured for drizzle-kit chain; no DDL to apply here' if safe, OR probeAndMarkApplied pattern for columns/tables that might not exist yet on fresh installs. brain.db baseline CRITICAL: R3 reported 261-line rebuild with PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF — baseline migration.sql must probe existing schema and NOT run the rebuild on populated DBs. Test on throwaway copies. After baseline reset confirmed — subsequent generates produce clean incremental diffs AND drizzle-kit check passes for all DBs. Execute one DB at a time: tasks first (simplest per R3), nexus second (already passes check per T1163), brain LAST (most complex). Do NOT proceed if any DB can't be made probe-and-skip safe — escalate to HITL.

tasks.title: CLI: cleo backup inspect command (manifest-only streaming read)
tasks.description: Implement packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/backup-inspect.ts as the cleo backup inspect <bundle> subcommand. For encrypted bundles: report encryption status and exit 0 without prompting passphrase. For plain bundles: stream-extract only the first tar entry (manifest.json), verify integrity.manifestHash, validate against embedded schemas/manifest-v1.json (not extracted from bundle), print structured report from spec §5.3, compare DB schemaVersions to local, emit [OK]/[OLDER]/[NEWER] per DB. Must complete in under 100ms for typical bundles. Zero disk writes. Register on backup.ts. Add inspect.test.ts. Files: backup-inspect.ts (new) + backup.ts (register only).

tasks.title: BUG CRITICAL: Project selection cookie ignored — all pages always read cwd .cleo/ paths
tasks.description: Root cause: getBrainDb/getTasksDb/getNexusDb/getConduitDb/getSignaldockDb in packages/studio/src/lib/server/db/connections.ts always resolve to the studio process cwd via getBrainDbPath() etc from cleo-home.ts. They IGNORE the project cookie. Fix requires: (1) hooks.server.ts handle reads cookie via getActiveProjectId, calls resolveProjectContext from project-context.ts, sets event.locals.projectCtx (or default ctx). (2) Update connections.ts each getter to accept optional ProjectContext: when present use ctx.brainDbPath/tasksDbPath, when absent use current global behavior. (3) Update all 20 callers (page.server.ts and api/*/+server.ts) to use event.locals.projectCtx via destructuring from PageServerLoad / RequestHandler signature.
acceptance: Switching project in header selector causes ALL pages to load from selected project's brain.db / tasks.db / conduit.db
hooks.server.ts handles cookie -> resolves project context -> sets event.locals.projectCtx
connections.ts accepts ProjectContext for path resolution (cwd fallback when no context)
All 20 page server loads + API endpoints use event.locals.projectCtx
app.d.ts declares Locals.projectCtx type
Test: switch project, confirm data differs
Build green
Type-safe

tasks.title: CLI: cleo restore finalize command
tasks.description: Implement packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/restore-finalize.ts as the cleo restore finalize subcommand. Behavior from spec §5.4: (1) if no .cleo/restore-conflicts.md exists, log no-op and exit 0; (2) parse conflict report for fields with 'RESOLVED:' prefix; (3) apply each resolved field to its target JSON file on disk; (4) archive conflict report to .cleo/restore-conflicts-<ISO-timestamp>.md.finalized; (5) remove .cleo/restore-conflicts.md; (6) log 'Finalized <N> conflict resolutions. Conflict report archived.' If no manually resolved fields found, print instruction and exit 0 without archiving. Register on restore.ts. Add restore-finalize.test.ts. Files: restore-finalize.ts (new) + restore.ts (register only).
acceptance: No restore-conflicts.md: exits 0 with 'No pending restore conflicts' message

Conflict report with unresolved manual-review fields: prints instruction to edit; does not archive
cleo restore finalize is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

tasks.title: PSYCHE E4: Governed Execution Pipelines — DSL contract enforcement for .cant/.cantbook (OpenProse-pattern: requires/ensures + error_handlers + thin-agent + hybrid graph)
tasks.description: CLEO has playbook runtime (ADR-053 state machine + HMAC resume tokens + externalized SQLite state) which is close to OpenProse-pattern Governed Execution Pipelines but contract enforcement is partial. Dependencies: E2 (playbook-architect meta-agent must exist before strict contract enforcement). Decision needed: strict cutover vs opt-in migration for existing .cantbooks? Per ADR-053 this is a state machine invariant, so strict cutover with migration tool recommended.

tasks.title: cleo agent remove --global and --force: safety-gated global deletion
tasks.description: Add --global and --force-global flags to cleo agent remove in packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/agent.ts per spec §5.2. Without --global: removes only project_agent_refs row from conduit.db (detach semantics per Q4=C). With --global: calls AgentRegistryAccessor.removeGlobal(); pre-check scans known project roots for project_agent_refs rows referencing the agent — if any found, prints WARNING listing affected projects and exits 1 unless --force is passed. With --global --force: bypasses cross-project scan warning; proceeds with global agents row deletion. Post-deletion dangling soft FKs are logged at WARN level by accessor on next read. Add integration tests TC-085, TC-086, TC-087. Files: packages/cleo/src/cli/commands/agent.ts.

tasks.title: T-KNOWLEDGE-FIRST-CITIZEN: TASKS + BRAIN + DOCS as complete knowledge system (zero markdown handoff dependency)
tasks.description: OPERATOR-MANDATED 2026-04-29 strategic goal: NO orchestrator EVER needs to read .cleo/agent-outputs/*.md or any other markdown for project state. The complete knowledge transfer surface must be CLEO's three pillars: TASKS (tasks.db — what), BRAIN (brain.db — why/learnings), DOCS (cleo docs — references/attachments). cleo docs is currently underused — agents don't attach context docs to tasks; ADRs live as raw markdown not registered in docs; agent-outputs/ pile up as untracked artifacts. Fresh-agent test 2026-04-29 caught the gap: even with redirect stubs on the deprecated handoff files, a future orchestrator might encounter a NEW markdown file (e.g. session report) and read it as state. The goal is that any orchestrator with access to .cleo/ — and only that — can pick up and go with 100% accuracy.
acceptance: T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-1 ADR migration: every ADR in docs/adr/ registered via cleo docs add; cleo docs list --task <id> returns relevant ADRs
T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-2 agent-output absorption: existing .cleo/agent-outputs/*.md files audited; substantive content migrated to BRAIN observations + cleo docs attachments; redundant files archived/deleted
T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-3 cleo docs first-class in spawn prompt: orchestrate spawn auto-attaches relevant docs to subagent's task via cleo docs

T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-4 session-debrief enrichment: cleo session end auto-creates BRAIN observations of type 'session-summary' linked to all tasks completed/created in the session — beyond just handoffJson text field
T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-5 cleo briefing reads from all 3 pillars: tasks (state) + brain (rationale) + docs (references); current briefing only reads tasks+brain
T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-6 forbid markdown writes to .cleo/agent-outputs/ as state: lint rule that fails CI if a new .md is added to agent-outputs/ without a corresponding BRAIN observation or task attachment

T-KNOWLEDGE-V1-7 fresh-agent acceptance test: independent orchestrator with NO markdown access (read-only block on .md in agent-outputs) recovers full project state from cleo briefing + cleo docs + cleo memory alone — must score 10/10
Result: any orchestrator on a fresh checkout can run cleo briefing → cleo docs list → cleo memory find and have complete project context. No markdown spelunking ever needed.

tasks.title: Wave R: Research — cross-machine backup portability formats
tasks.description: Research phase of T311 RCASD pipeline. Audit current backup mechanism, survey portable archive formats, define manifest requirements, and recommend CLI verb surface. Output is a structured report that informs ADR-038 + Specification + Decomposition phases. Read-only investigation.
acceptance: Investigation report at .cleo/research/T311-backup-portability-audit.md
Section 1: Current VACUUM INTO backup mechanism (from v2026.4.10/4.11) — file format, rotation, metadata
Section 2: Portable archive format options — tar.gz, zip, cleo-native .cleobak — pros/cons for cross-platform (Linux/macOS/Windows)
Section 3: Manifest schema — what metadata must travel with a backup (source machine fingerprint, cleo version, DB schema versions, timestamps, checksums, compression method)
Section 4: Integrity validation — SHA-256 checksums, SQLite integrity_check on restore, source-fingerprint validation, schema compatibility check
Section 5: Security considerations — should exports be encrypted/signed (agent credentials in signaldock.db are sensitive)
Section 6: Restore modes — fresh install vs merge into existing install, conflict resolution for merge
Section 7: Candidate CLI verb surfaces: export/import/inspect, --scope flag, --encrypt flag
Section 8: RCASD next-step recommendations for Consensus + ADR-038 + Spec
Report informs ADR-038 authorship

tasks.title: PERF: context.ts/impact.ts/clusters.ts load ALL nexus rows into memory before filtering — 3.6x slower than gitnexus (2.4s vs 0.67s)
tasks.description: T1042 audit perf fix: context.ts/impact.ts/clusters.ts each call db.select().from(nexusSchema.nexusNodes).all() loading ALL 89k+ rows from shared global nexus.db (4,668 projects mostly test pollution) then filtering in-memory. Causes 2.4s p50 context queries vs gitnexus 0.67s — 3.6x slower. Fix: add WHERE project_id = ? filter to all three queries; use SQL JOINs for BFS traversal instead of building adjacency maps from full-table scans.
acceptance: context query uses SQL WHERE project_id = ? filter
impact BFS uses indexed SQL traversal not in-memory
clusters uses SQL filter
p50 query time <500ms on openclaw (vs current 2.4s)

tasks.title: FIX P1: GPU mode blank canvas — .lb-canvas missing height:100% (cosmos.gl reads 0px in constructor)
tasks.description: Lead 2 council finding (T662): cosmos.gl v2.0.0-beta.26 reads canvas.clientHeight synchronously in its constructor. .lb-canvas in LivingBrainCosmograph.svelte has width:100% but missing height:100%. tasks.title: Implement pipeline stage rollback — testing failure routes back to implementation
tasks.description: CleoOS currently has forward-only pipeline stages. When a testing gate fails, the task should be able to route back to implementation for rework. Implement: (1) a rollback transition type in lifecycleTransitions, (2) logic in the stage advancement handler to re-enter a previous stage on gate failure, (3) update lifecycleStages.status from completed back to in_progress for the target stage. Must preserve the audit trail — the original completion and the rollback are both recorded. This directly unblocks the rollback-on-test-failure scenario.
acceptance: Stage can transition backward on gate failure
lifecycleTransitions records rollback with from/to stages
Original completion preserved in audit trail
Re-entered stage status set to in_progress
rollback-on-test-failure scenario score improves to 0.7+

tasks.title: Wire challenge loop — ct-validator re-execution on gate failure
tasks.description: Connect ct-validator skill to the orchestration layer so that gate failures trigger automatic re-execution. When a lifecycleGateResult records result=fail, the orchestrator should: (1) route the task back to the appropriate stage (uses pipeline rollback from T466), (2) dispatch the ct-task-executor subagent for rework, (3) re-run validation after rework. Limit to max 3 retry rounds to prevent infinite loops. Record each round in the gate results with incrementing round numbers. This unblocks the challenge-loop-validator scenario.

tasks.title: EPIC: CANT runtime completeness — activate WorkflowExecutor, fix all gaps, wire createRuntime
tasks.description: Epic created 2026-04-07 in response to T262 audit findings. Sibling to T261 (Pi v2+v3). Scope: activate the dead WorkflowExecutor in @cleocode/core cant/ namespace (1489 LOC across workflow-executor.ts, approval.ts, discretion.ts, context-builder.ts, parallel-runner.ts, types.ts — all exported, zero production callers). Rust source of truth (crates/cant-core/src/dsl/ast.rs + ast_orchestration.rs) defines 16 workflow Statement types: Binding, Expression, Directive, Property, Comment, Session, Parallel, Conditional, Repeat, ForLoop, LoopUntil, TryCatch, ApprovalGate, Pipeline, PipeStep, Output. The TS WorkflowExecutor has 3 missing handlers (Expression, Property, PipeStep), 3 scaffold stubs (executeSession, executePipeline, executeDirective return {stub:true}), a broken condition evaluator (workflow-executor.ts:562-564 returns true for all non-discretion conditions), and a hardcoded DefaultDiscretionEvaluator (discretion.ts:46-49). Rust crates have ZERO TODOs. This epic tracks closing ALL gaps in the same PR as T273–T276 so T275 Pi extension and T287 cleo agent start fix can actually execute workflows. Labelled pi-epic-t261 for cross-reference. Blocks T273, T275.

tasks.title: EPIC: CleoOS Database Topology + Lifecycle (v2026.4.11)
tasks.description: Complete the work started in ADR-013 §9 by establishing the full database topology (4 DBs × 2 tiers) as a first-class architectural concern. v2026.4.10 only closed the project-tier data-loss vector for tasks.db + brain.db. This epic addresses 9 remaining gaps: (1) nested package .cleo/ dirs still tracking DBs + WAL sidecars in git (T5158 still live in nested scope), (2) cleo auto-creates nested .cleo/ instead of walking up, (3) backup registry missing signaldock.db at project tier, (4) global nexus.db has zero rotating-backup coverage, (5) signaldock.db has no global-tier identity story, (6) legacy files at global tier (workspace.db + .bak + nexus-pre-cleo.db.bak) pure cruft, (7) stray .cleo/cleo.db (0-byte) + tracked .cleo/tasks_test.db, (8) stray project-tier .cleo/nexus.db (code says nexus is global-only), (9) paths.ts:5 doc comment stale. Architectural contract after v2026.4.11: project tier = tasks.db + brain.db + signaldock.db + config.json + project-info.json; global tier via env-paths XDG = nexus.db + global signaldock.db + machine-key + existing global config. 13 atomic tasks across 6 waves. Split signaldock dual-scope into T-D1a (schema+accessor) + T-D1b (migration+backup) to reduce risk. Critical path: ADR-036 → walk-up fix → global backup → signaldock schema → signaldock migration → cross-machine export → integration tests → release. Baseline: v2026.4.10 at commit 621cb656, tagged + pushed + GitHub Release live. Target: v2026.4.11 release with 13 closed children.
acceptance: ADR-036 written and synced documenting the full 4-DB × 2-tier topology, scaffolding rules, backup mechanism, recovery procedures, and cross-machine portability

tasks.title: EPIC: Conduit + Signaldock Separation (project conduit.db + global signaldock.db)
tasks.description: Stand-alone RCASD-IVTR epic for the conduit/signaldock separation refactor called out in owner's Q1 decision for T299's T-D1 scope. The current project-level .cleo/signaldock.db needs to be renamed to conduit.db (matches the existing packages/core/src/conduit/ code organization and the conceptual framing that conduit = comms at project level scope). Simultaneously, a NEW global-tier signaldock.db at $XDG_DATA_HOME/cleo/signaldock.db holds the canonical agent identity registry (so agents persist machine-wide instead of per-project). Project conduit.db references global signaldock by ID. This is substantial enough to need the full RCASD-IVTR lifecycle: Research phase audits current signaldock usage and conduit module organization; Consensus phase aligns on the migration strategy; ADR-037 documents the final decision; Specification phase writes concrete contracts; Decomposition produces atomic tasks. Implementation + Verification + Testing + Release follow. Blocks on T299 (v2026.4.11) so the database topology foundation is in place first. Targets v2026.4.12 or later release.

tasks.title: EPIC: Cross-Machine CleoOS Backup Export/Import
tasks.description: Stand-alone epic for cross-machine backup portability called out in owner's Q2 decision. v2026.4.10 shipped VACUUM INTO snapshots; v2026.4.11 ships global-tier backup; this epic adds the export/import layer that makes snapshots portable between machines. Use case: user sets up CleoOS on a new machine and wants to restore their global nexus.db from a backup taken on their old machine. Scope: tarball-based export/import with manifest.json + fingerprint validation + integrity checks. Format must work for both project-tier and global-tier backups. Needs full RCASD before implementation because the format decision (tarball vs zip vs cleo-native), signing model, and compression choices have long-term compatibility implications. Targets v2026.4.13 or later release.
acceptance: Full RCASD pipeline completed: Research (audit existing backup mechanism + portability options) → Consensus → ADR-038 → Specification → Decomposition
New ADR-038 CleoOS Backup Portability + Cross-Machine Restore written and synced

cleo backup import <path> unpacks and restores the tarball into the correct tier (project vs global)
Restore validates manifest schema + source fingerprint + integrity_check before committing
--scope project
global flag supports both tiers
Export format is portable across machines and platforms (Linux/macOS/Windows)
Manifest includes: backup timestamp, source machine fingerprint (hash of machine-key), cleo version at export time, schema versions of each bundled DB, list of files with SHA-256 checksums
Round-trip test: export from machine A → copy tarball → import to machine B → verify all data preserved and schemas match
Integration with the VACUUM INTO backup mechanism from v2026.4.10/4.11
IVTR: Implementation → Verification → Testing (unit + integration) → Release as v2026.4.13 or later

tasks.title: EPIC: CleoOS Agentic Execution Layer — Pi-wrapped TUI + Parallel Delegation + Worker Isolation
tasks.description: Umbrella epic completing the CleoOS agentic execution layer vision per ULTRAPLAN and the 2026-04-08 agentic-layer proposal. Delivers a batteries-included cleoos binary that installs via npm i -g @cleocode/cleo-os, wraps Pi as the canonical harness, enforces three-tier orchestrator/lead/worker hierarchy, routes requests via CANT-declared classification, delegates parallel-safe work via fanout, isolates every worker in a git worktree with CWD and credential binding, and enforces path-scoped domain ACLs via Pi tool_call hooks. Closes ULTRAPLAN Waves 3, 7, 8, 9 and adds two new waves (Domain ACLs, Protocol hygiene + ct-master-tac plugin) that were not in the original plan. Foundational waves 0, 1, 5, 6 appear mostly shipped and receive an audit-and-gap-file pass.

tasks.title: Review & implement Sitar-inspired Config Platform
tasks.description:
Round back and review the Sitar-inspired CLEO Config Platform architecture documented in docs/architecture/config-platform.md. This epic tracks the review, refinement, and staged implementation of a unified configuration management platform for CLEO, modeled on Airbnb's Sitar system.

## Background

Airbnb's Sitar is a dynamic configuration platform with 4 layers: developer-facing (GitHub PRs), control plane (orchestrated rollouts), data plane (versioned storage), and sidecar clients (local cache + hot reload). It uses staged rollouts, typed schema validation, separated control/data planes, emergency bypass with audit trails, and feature flags.

We designed a local-first mapping of Sitar onto the CLEO monorepo on 2026-04-05. The full architecture document lives at docs/architecture/config-platform.md.

## Core Mapping

- Sitar tenants → CLEO config domains (mapped to 10 canonical dispatch domains)
- Control plane → core/config/control-plane.ts (7-step orchestration)
- Data plane → new config.db (4th SQLite DB) with append-only versioned entries
- Sidecar → runtime/services/config-watcher.ts
- Staged rollout → scope progression (agent:id → project → global)
- Emergency bypass → --emergency --reason with auto-expiry
- Feature flags → config_flags table + flag-resolver middleware
- Schema validation → 4-layer pipeline mirroring existing verification gates

## Key Design Decisions

1. config.db as 4th database — separation of concerns
2. config.json becomes materialized view — backward compat preserved
3. Append-only log — rollback by flipping is_current flags
4. 3 new middleware: flag-resolver, config-scope-resolver, config-gate
5. No new packages — extends contracts, core, cleo, runtime only

## Review Scope

Before implementation, review and refine:

1. Does the 4-layer validation pipeline duplicate or conflict with existing verification gates?
2. Is config.db the right SSoT approach, or should we extend tasks.db or brain.db?
3. How does this interact with the CANT DSL initiative and Pi pivot?
4. Should feature flags live in LAFS instead of config platform?
5. Emergency bypass auto-expiry — is 24h the right default?
6. How does ConfigWatcher interact with the Conductor Loop (CleoOS Phase 3)?
7. Alignment with RCASD-IVTR+C lifecycle model
8. Migration path for existing .cleo/config.json files

## Phases (per architecture doc)

- Phase 1: Foundation — contracts + config.db schema
- Phase 2: Control Plane — validation + versioning + git-sync
- Phase 3: CLI Surface — commands + middleware
- Phase 4: Feature Flags
- Phase 5: Runtime Distribution — ConfigWatcher
- Phase 6: Staged Rollout + Emergency Bypass

tasks.title: EPIC P0: Project-agnostic cleo release pipeline — Release phase of LOOM/IVTR
tasks.description: USER DIAGNOSIS (2026-04-16 post-v2026.4.70 ship): cleo release ship|prepare|show|etc. is partially functional and hardcoded to the cleocode monorepo. Downstream projects using CLEO cannot drive their own release pipeline through 'cleo release'. The Release phase of LOOM/IVTR (the 4th 'R' in RCASD-IVTR) must become a project-agnostic, batteries-included, CI/CD-integrated tool that closes the agent-accountability loop. Composes T810 IVTR state (Release = phase after Test passes) + T768 programmatic gates (release pre-flight gates) + T771 cleo docs (CHANGELOG evidence attachment) + T760 commit↔task association. Evidence: live test of 'cleo release ship 2026.4.70 --epic T760 --noBump --dryRun' failed at step 1/8 with 'Release v2026.4.70 not found' despite auto-prepare reporting success; v2026.4.66-69 were all shipped WITHOUT using cleo release (bypassed via raw git tag + GitHub Actions). cleo release list only shows v2026.4.25-26 rolled-back releases.

semver
custom, tagPrefix, gitWorkflow direct
pr, registries npm
crates
docker
none, prerelease channels)
RELEASE-02: Auto-generate CHANGELOG from git log since last tag — for each commit, parse epic/task IDs from message, group by epic, include REQ-IDs + evidence sha256 refs via cleo docs
RELEASE-03: IVTR gate enforcement — cleo release ship rejects when any task in release epic has ivtr_state.currentPhase != 'released' OR gate failures; --force escape-hatch with loud owner warning
RELEASE-04: PR-first mode — opens draft PR with auto-body (CHANGELOG section + tasks table + evidence links), awaits review, merges on approval, then tags
RELEASE-05: Actual rollback — cleo release rollback deletes tag, reverts commit, removes release record, optional npm unpublish/deprecate
RELEASE-06: Integration test on a minimal downstream CLEO-using project (not cleocode itself) — proves zero hardcoded assumptions
RELEASE-07: cleo release ship is wired into IVTR as the final phase — after cleo orchestrate ivtr <epic> --release succeeds (I+V+T evidence complete), cleo release ship is auto-suggested as next step

Scope: (1) Playbook runtime state machine (W4-10), (2) 3 starter playbooks — rcasd/ivtr/release.cantbook (W4-11), (3) cleo playbook CLI domain (W4-12), (4) Thin-agent runtime enforcer (W4-14/W4-15), (5) cleo orchestrate approve/reject/pending CLI (W4-17), (6) composeSpawnPayload full integration into orchestrate-engine.ts spawn path (W3-7), (7) docs/architecture/orchestration-flow.md + Mermaid diagram (W3-8), (8) SDK UNIFICATION: deprecate @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk + @openai/agents in packages/adapters/, standardize on Vercel AI SDK (ai v6.0.168) already used in packages/core/src/memory/ for LLM calls + tool use + generateObject. Provider abstraction via @ai-sdk/anthropic + @ai-sdk/openai. CLEO retains its own orchestration primitives (composeSpawnPayload, playbook runtime, thin-agent, registry) — SDK is the LLM bridge, not the orchestrator. (9) Bump SDK versions to latest: @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk 0.2.108→0.2.113 (if kept), @ai-sdk/anthropic 3.0.69→3.0.71. (10) Harness interop tests: sandbox playbook execution across Claude Code, OpenCode, and Pi harnesses to verify provider-agnostic operation. Rationale: v2026.4.86 shipped foundation primitives (contracts, schema, parser, state, policy, HMAC tokens, composer APIs) that are all type-safe and real-tested but NOT WIRED into the live spawn path. T910 wires them and consolidates the SDK surface.

3 starter playbooks (rcasd, ivtr, release) execute successfully end-to-end on real epic

tasks.title: EPIC: Clean Code SSoT Reconciliation — CONDUIT/BRAIN/memory/casts/contracts
tasks.description: Foundation reconciliation epic born from T910 research. Five converging drift vectors: (1) CONDUIT folded under orchestrate per ADR-042 despite being a 444-line first-class handler + its own protocol spec (ADR-042 invariant already dead, we're at 14 domains not 10); (2) BRAIN/memory naming inverted relative to operator's mental model (BRAIN=super-graph wrapping memory+nexus+tasks+conduit+signaldock; memory=observations/patterns/decisions/learnings+tiers); (3) 753 latent type casts (579 param casts in dispatch/domains/) from monorepo merge 2026-03-18 that never wired to the existing typed contracts; (4) 5/5 sampled contract\u2194impl drift bugs including OrchestrateSpawnParams with ZERO field overlap; (5) Studio HTTP surface has zero input validation. Research evidence: .cleo/agent-outputs/T910-reconciliation/{conduit-collision,dispatch-cast-audit,brain-memory-reconciliation,living-brain-architecture,type-safety-audit}.md. Operator-approved reconciliation plan: ADD brain domain + EXTRACT @cleocode/brain package + ATOMIC HTTP rename + A+C cast strategy (compile-time adapter now, zod runtime later as separate epic). T949 Studio viz merge depends on this epic shipping first (T949 builds on reconciled contracts).

tasks.title: Epic: T-BRANCH-LOCK — harness-agnostic agent branch protection
tasks.description: P0 platform-level guardrail: agents spawned by any orchestrator MUST be programmatically prevented from mutating git branch state on the orchestrator's working tree. Must work in ANY agent harness (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Codex, OpenHands, Pi, CleoOS, custom scripts, future harnesses). Root cause of T1093 near-disaster: T1097 retry agent ran 'git checkout feat/t1013-fill-gaps' at 20:19:05 PDT, moving orchestrator off main. User-identified. Must never happen again. Core approach: git worktree isolation at CLEO orchestrate-spawn layer (git is harness-agnostic). Each spawned agent gets its own worktree on its own branch; orchestrator stays on main untouched.
acceptance: Harness-agnostic solution identified and proven
Git worktree isolation integrated into cleo orchestrate spawn
Agent working tree != orchestrator working tree
Agent cannot access orchestrator .git/HEAD
Agent commits flow back via cherry-pick or PR merge, never direct push to main
Proven working in Claude Code, with design verified applicable to Cursor/Aider/any shell harness

tasks.title: T-MSR: Migration System Remediation — end drizzle debt spiral across 5 DBs
tasks.description: End the drizzle migration debt-spiral at v2026.4.107. State: T1118 trailing statement-breakpoint still crashes fresh DBs despite T1137 fix attempt; duplicate T1126 folders (000001+000002 timestamp collision); snapshot chain broken since 2026-03-24 (9 of 15 tasks migrations have no snapshot.json); drizzle.config.ts points at scratchpad out=./drizzle/migrations/*; drizzle-kit still beta.19 while drizzle-orm is beta.22; custom reconciler in migration-manager.ts absorbs every edge case as a new patch. FIVE DBs in scope: project-level TASKS+BRAIN+CONDUIT, system-level NEXUS+SIGNALDOCK, plus TELEMETRY (core-only). Owner leans Path B (repair drizzle-kit flow). Formal RCASD decides Path A vs Path B with evidence. Adopts T1141 (trailing-breakpoint generator hardening) as child. Three-layer package architecture must be respected: core=SDK SSoT, cleo=CLI wrapper, cleo-os=harness that inherits transitively.

tasks.title: Pump #2: Close parent-closure-without-atom (epics complete with verification=null)
tasks.description: Council verification audit (T-COUNCIL-VERIFICATION-2026-04-24) identified a reproducible failure-mode pump: parent epics close with verification=null as long as children carry evidence, violating ADR-051 at the parent granularity. Instance count: 7 epics in v2026.4.133 (T1148, T1147, T1075, T1259, T1261, T1145, T1146). Future orchestrators clone the pattern. Fix: cleo complete <epicId> must enforce either (a) direct evidence atoms on the parent, OR (b) a formal inheritance rule (e.g., merkle hash of children.evidence, per Expansionist F3) that produces a provenance-bearing parent verification record. Archival should be rejected for parent epics that claim completion without either path.

tasks.title: Epic: T-DISPATCH-INFER eliminate dispatch-contracts type drift via Core inference
tasks.description:
Owner directive 2026-04-25: 'No workarounds EVER. Do the whole thing. Do it right. Boil the ocean.' Option A from architecture review.

Current state (v2026.4.144 / commit cc655cd48): dispatch files (15,815 LOC across 22 domains) are inverted in size vs contracts (11,136 LOC). Each dispatch domain hand-imports 50+ *Params + *Result types from @cleocode/contracts to satisfy TypedDomainHandler<O extends TypedOpRecord>. Contracts hand-shadowed Core function signatures; dispatch hand-shadowed contracts — three places to update for one verb. CI failed v2026.4.143/.144 because contracts didn't keep up with dispatch (104 missing exports surfaced).

Option A: Core's TypeScript signatures ARE the contract. Dispatch infers Params/Result via Parameters<typeof core.fn>[0] + Awaited<ReturnType<typeof core.fn>>. Contracts keeps only wire-format types. Drift class structurally impossible.

Scope: 9 dispatch domains (admin, check, conduit, nexus, pipeline, playbook, sentient, session, tasks). Plus contracts cleanup, ADR, regression-prevention lint, full tests, green release CI.

NON-NEGOTIABLE: No any/unknown/as-unknown-as-X shortcuts. No test mutes. No inline types in dispatch. Quality gates green before completion.
acceptance: All 9 dispatch domains import operations from packages/core/src/<domain>/* and infer Params/Result via Parameters<typeof fn> + Awaited<ReturnType<typeof fn>> — zero per-op Params/Result type imports from @cleocode/contracts in dispatch files
Helper OpsFromCore<T> added to packages/cleo/src/dispatch/adapters/typed.ts with TSDoc + unit tests; integrated with TypedDomainHandler<O extends TypedOpRecord>
packages/contracts/src/operations/* stripped of redundant per-op Params/Result types — only wire-format types remain (LafsEnvelope, LafsPage, LafsError, shared domain enums)
ADR filed under docs/adr/ documenting the new pattern + rationale (drift elimination class) + migration guide
biome lint rule rejecting top-level type/interface declarations under packages/cleo/src/dispatch/domains/* — dispatch becomes thin routers only
Repo-wide tsc -b exit 0; biome ci . exit 0; pnpm run build exit 0; pnpm run test 0 failures (no regressions)
Dispatch domain files reduced 50%+ in LOC (15.8k → ~8k target)
Release v2026.4.145 (or next clean tag) shipped with green CI workflow on GitHub Actions

tasks.title: Epic: T-CORE-CONTRACTS-SSOT align Core API with Contracts
tasks.description:
Owner directive 2026-04-25 (after T1435 Option A friction analysis): 'Core + Contracts MUST be the SSoT. Stop bandaid drifting. Our entire system MUST be type safe with same types from contracts to cli. Core usage must be the SSoT entry into our systems operations.'

T1435 Option A failed because Core's internal API used different field names than the wire format. The fix is NOT to translate in dispatch — it's to ALIGN Core's API with Contracts so both reference the same types.

Architecture:
  packages/contracts/src/operations/<domain>.ts → SSoT for op shapes
        ↓
  packages/core/src/<domain>/*.ts → Core fn signature uses contract types directly
        ↓
  packages/cleo/src/dispatch/domains/<domain>.ts → Thin pass-through via OpsFromCore inference

The OpsFromCore helper (T1436) is correct — it's the inference primitive. T1435 mistake was inferring from a Core API that didn't match the contract; T1449 FIXES Core to match.

Scope: 9 dispatch domains × ~30 ops avg = ~250 Core function signature refactors; hundreds of internal Core call-site updates; comprehensive test verification; ADR; CI gate; release. Per owner: time/complexity/fatigue NOT excuses.
acceptance: Every Core function in scope has signature (projectRoot, params: <Domain><Op>Params): Promise<<Domain><Op>Result> using contract types directly
All 9 dispatch domains become thin pass-throughs via OpsFromCore<typeof coreOps>; zero translation logic in dispatch handlers
Field-name divergence resolved (contract wire format wins; Core renames internally; all internal callers updated)
Drift structurally impossible: changing contract type breaks Core signature at compile time and CI rejects
All tests pass; biome+tsc+build green; dispatch LOC reduced 50%+
ADR documenting the SSoT layering + migration recipe + escape hatch
Release v2026.4.146+ shipped with green CI

tasks.title: BUG: surprisal-tree randomProjectionVector NaN propagation — randomBytes-as-Float32 produces NaN
tasks.description: Worker investigation 2026-05-05: randomProjectionVector() in packages/core/src/memory/surprisal-tree.ts called randomBytes(dim*4) reinterpreted as Float32Array. IEEE 754 bit patterns from crypto bytes can be NaN/Infinity. NaN chain: Float32Array(randomBytes) → 'v*2-1' preserves NaN → 'Math.sqrt(NaN) || 1' evaluates to 1 (silent mask, NaN is falsy) → projection vector with NaN components → all dot products NaN → 'NaN<=NaN' AND 'NaN>NaN' both false → degenerate split → leaf node at depth 0 → 'SELECT MAX(depth)' returns 0 → specialists.test.ts:245 assertion fails. Failed in v2026.5.25, v2026.5.26, v2026.5.27 CI runs. Fix: replaced randomBytes-Float32 with Box-Muller transform on Math.random() (proper standard-normal). Removed node:crypto import.
acceptance: specialists.test.ts buildSurprisalTree test passes 5/5 sequential runs
no node:crypto randomBytes pattern in randomProjectionVector

tasks.title: Lead-tier conduit supervisor: orchestrator->lead->workers swarm pattern
tasks.description: Wave A foundation: enable swarm orchestration. Today orchestrators spawn workers directly which doesn't scale beyond ~5 concurrent and floods orchestrator context with raw worker outputs. This epic builds the Lead-tier supervisor pattern: orchestrator -> Phase Lead (subagent role=orchestrator) -> N workers (role=leaf). Lead subscribes to epic-<T>.wave-<n> topic, workers publish status events, Lead aggregates and rolls up. Substrate exists: conduit subscribeTopic+publishToTopic+parseTopicName already shipped (T1252). a2a-topic tests already validate Lead A / Lead B fanout. Missing: ct-lead skill, Lead aggregation helper, ADR formalizing 3-tier model, cleo orchestrate roll-up command.

Worker status events use canonical schema: kind=status, payload={taskId,phase,gateProgress,evidence}
Lead aggregation: cleo orchestrate roll-up <epic> queries pipeline_manifest+conduit topics for unified swarm view
